G4455.kjv: at any time, + never (…to any man), + yet, never man

G4456.def: apparently from (a kind of stone); to petrify, i.e. (figuratively) to indurate (render stupid or callous)

G4458.def: adverb from the base of G4225 (πού); an enclitic particle of indefiniteness of manner; somehow or anyhow; used only in composition.

G4459.def: adverb from the base of G4226 (ποῦ); an interrogative particle of manner; in what way? (sometimes the question is indirect, how?); also as exclamation, how much!.

G4460.def: of Hebrew origin (G7343); Raab (i.e. Rachab), a Canaanitess.

G4461.def: of Hebrew origin (H07227 with pronominal suffix); my master, i.e Rabbi, as an official title of honor.

G4462.def: of Chaldee origin; corresponding to G4461 (ῥαββί)

G4463.def: from G4464 (ῥάβδος); to strike with a stick, i.e. bastinado.

G4464.def: from the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω); a stick or wand (as a cudgel, a cane or a baton of royalty)

G4465.def: from G4464 (ῥάβδος) and G2192 (ἔχω); a rod- (the Latin fasces) holder, i.e. a Roman lictor (constable or executioner)

G4466.def: of Hebrew origin (H07466); Ragau (i.e. Reu), a patriarch.

G4467.def: from a comparative of (easy, i.e. reckless) and G2041 (ἔργον); easy-going behavior, i.e. (by extension) a crime.

G4468.def: from the same as G4467 (ῥᾳδιούργημα); recklessness, i.e. (by extension) malignity.

G4469.def: of Chaldee origin (compare H07386); O empty one, i.e. thou worthless (as a term of utter vilification)

G4470.def: from G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); a "rag," i.e. piece of cloth.

G4471.def: of Hebrew origin (H07414); Rama (i.e. Ramah), a place in Palestine.

G4472.def: from a derivative of (to sprinkle); to render besprinkled, i.e. asperse (ceremonially or figuratively)

G4474.def: from a derivative of a primary (to let fall, "rap"); to slap.
G4474.kjv: smite (with the palm of the hand)

G4475.kjv: (+ strike with the) palm of the hand, smite with the hand

G4476.def: from a primary (to sew; perhaps rather akin to the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω) through the idea of puncturing); a needle.

G4477.def: from the same as G4460 (Ῥαάβ); Rachab, a Canaanitess.

G4478.def: of Hebrew origin (H07354); Rachel, the wife of Jacob.

G4479.def: of Hebrew origin (H07259); Rebecca (i.e. Ribkah), the wife of Isaac.

G4480.def: of Latin origin; a rheda, i.e. four-wheeled carriage (wagon for riding)

G4481.def: by incorrect transliteration for a word of Hebrew origin (H03594); Remphan (i.e. Kijun), an Egyptian idol.

G4482.def: a primary verb; for some tenses of which a prolonged form is used to flow ("run"; as water)

G4483.def: perhaps akin (or identical) with G4482 (ῥέω) (through the idea of pouring forth); to utter, i.e. speak or say.

G4484.def: of Latin origin; Rhegium, a place in Italy.

G4485.def: from G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); something torn, i.e. a fragment (by implication and abstractly, a fall)

G4486.def: both prolonged forms of (which appears only in certain forms, and is itself probably a strengthened form of (see in G2608 (κατάγνυμι))) to "break," "wreck" or "crack", i.e. (especially) to sunder (by separation of the parts; G2608 (κατάγνυμι) being its intensive (with the preposition in composition), and G2352 (θραύω) a shattering to minute fragments; but not a reduction to the constituent particles, like G3089 (λύω)) or disrupt, lacerate; by implication, to convulse (with spasms); figuratively, to give vent to joyful emotions.

G4487.def: from G4483 (ῥέω); an utterance (individually, collectively or specially),; by implication, a matter or topic (especially of narration, command or dispute); with a negative naught whatever.

G4488.def: probably of Hebrew origin (apparently for H07509); Resa (i.e. Rephajah), an Israelite.

G4489.def: from G4483 (ῥέω); a speaker, i.e. (by implication) a forensic advocate.

G4490.def: adverb from a derivative of G4483 (ῥέω); out-spokenly, i.e. distinctly.

G4491.def: apparently a primary word; a "root" (literally or figuratively)

G4492.def: from G4491 (ῥίζα); to root (figuratively, become stable)

G4493.def: from G4496 (ῥίπτω); a jerk (of the eye, i.e. (by analogy) an instant)

G4494.def: from a derivative of G4496 (ῥίπτω) (meaning a fan or bellows); to breeze up, i.e. (by analogy) to agitate (into waves)

G4495.def: from a derivative of G4496 (ῥίπτω); to toss up.

G4496.def: a primary verb (perhaps rather akin to the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω), through the idea of sudden motion); to fling (properly, with a quick toss, thus differing from G906 (βάλλω), which denotes a deliberate hurl; and from (see in G1614 (ἐκτείνω)), which indicates an extended projection); by qualification, to deposit (as if a load); by extension, to disperse.

G4497.def: of Hebrew origin (H07346); Roboam (i.e. Rechobam), an Israelite.

G4498.def: probably for rhode (a rose); Rode, a servant girl.

G4499.def: probably from (a rose); Rhodus, an island of the Mediterranean.

G4500.def: adverb from a derivative of (a whir); whizzingly, i.e. with a crash.

G4501.def: probably of foreign origin; a sabre, i.e. a long and broad cutlass (any weapon of the kind, literally or figuratively)

G4502.def: of Hebrew origin (H07205); Ruben (i.e. Reuben), an Israelite.

G4503.def: of Hebrew origin (H07327); Ruth, a Moabitess.

G4504.def: of Latin origin; red; Rufus, a Christian.

G4505.def: prolongation from G4506 (ῥύομαι) in its original sense; an alley or avenue (as crowded)

G4506.def: middle voice of an obsolete verb, akin to G4482 (ῥέω) (through the idea of a current; compare G4511 (ῥύσις)); to rush or draw (for oneself), i.e. rescue.

G4508.def: from G4509 (ῥύπος); dirty, i.e. (relatively) cheap or shabby; morally, wicked.

G4509.def: of uncertain affinity; dirt, i.e. (morally) depravity.

G4510.def: from G4509 (ῥύπος); to soil, i.e. (intransitively) to become dirty (morally)

G4511.def: from G4506 (ῥύομαι) in the sense of its congener G4482 (ῥέω); a flux (of blood)

G4512.def: from G4506 (ῥύομαι); a fold (as drawing together), i.e. a wrinkle (especially on the face)

G4514.def: from G4516 (Ῥώμη); Romæan, i.e. Roman (as noun)

G4515.def: adverb from a presumed derivative of G4516 (Ῥώμη); Romaistically, i.e. in the Latin language.

G4516.def: from the base of G4517 (ῥώννυμι); strength; Roma, the capital of Italy.

G4517.def: prolongation from (to dart; probably akin to G4506 (ῥύομαι)); to strengthen, i.e. (impersonal passive) have health (as a parting exclamation, good-bye)

G4518.def: of Chaldee or (H07662 with pronominal suffix); thou hast left me; sabachthani (i.e. shebakthani), a cry of distress.

G4519.def: of Hebrew origin (H06635 in feminine plural); armies; sabaoth (i.e. tsebaoth), a military epithet of God.

G4520.def: from a derivative of G4521 (σάββατον); a "sabbatism", i.e. (figuratively) the repose of Christianity (as a type of heaven)

G4521.def: of Hebrew origin (H07676); the Sabbath (i.e. Shabbath), or day of weekly repose from secular avocations (also the observance or institution itself); by extension, a se'nnight, i.e. the interval between two Sabbaths; likewise the plural in all the above applications.

G4522.def: from a derivative of (to equip) meaning furniture, especially a pack-saddle (which in the East is merely a bag of netted rope); a "seine" for fishing.

G4523.def: probably from G4524 (Σαδώκ); a Sadducæan (i.e. Tsadokian), or follower of a certain heretical Israelite.

G4524.def: of Hebrew origin (H06659); Sadoc (i.e. Tsadok), an Israelite.

G4525.def: akin to G4579 (σείω); to wag (as a dog its tail fawningly), i.e. (generally) to shake (figuratively, disturb)

G4526.def: of Hebrew origin (H08242); "sack"-cloth, i.e. mohair (the material or garments made of it, worn as a sign of grief)

G4527.def: of Hebrew origin (H07974); Sala (i.e. Shelach), a patriarch.

G4528.def: of Hebrew origin (H07597); Salathiel (i.e. Shealtiel), an Israelite.

G4529.def: probably from G4535 (σάλος) (from the surge on the shore); Salamis, a place in Cyprus.

G4530.def: probably from the same as G4531 (σαλεύω); Salim, a place in Palestine.

G4531.def: from G4535 (σάλος); to waver, i.e. agitate, rock, topple or (by implication) destroy; figuratively, to disturb, incite.
G4531.kjv: move, shake (together), which can(-not) be shaken, stir up

G4532.def: of Hebrew origin (H08004); Salem (i.e. Shalem), a place in Palestine.

G4533.def: of Hebrew origin (H08012); Salmon, an Israelite.

G4534.def: perhaps of similar origin to G4529 (Σαλαμίς); Salmone, a place in Crete.

G4535.def: probably from the base of G4525 (σαίνω); a vibration, i.e. (specially), billow.

G4536.def: perhaps from G4535 (σάλος) (through the idea of quavering or reverberation); a trumpet.

G4537.def: from G4536 (σάλπιγξ); to trumpet, i.e. sound a blast (literally or figuratively)

G4539.def: probably of Hebrew origin (feminine from H07965); Salome (i.e. Shelomah), an Israelitess.

G4540.def: of Hebrew origin (H08111); Samaria (i.e. Shomeron), a city and region of Palestine.

G4541.def: from G4540 (Σαμάρεια); a Samarite, i.e. inhabitant of Samaria.

G4542.def: feminine of G4541 (Σαμαρείτης); a Samaritess, i.e. woman of Samaria.

G4543.def: from G4544 (Σάμος) and (Thrace); Samo-thrace (Samos of Thrace), an island in the Mediterranean.

G4544.def: of uncertain affinity; Samus, an island of the Mediterranean.

G4545.def: of Hebrew origin (H08050); Samuel (i.e. Shemuel), an Israelite.

G4546.def: of Hebrew origin (H08123); Sampson (i.e. Shimshon), an Israelite.

G4547.def: neuter of a derivative of (a "sandal"; of uncertain origin); a slipper or sole-pad.

G4549.def: of Hebrew origin (H07586); Saul (i.e. Shaul), the Jewish name of Paul.

G4550.def: from G4595 (σήπω); rotten, i.e. worthless (literally or morally)

G4551.def: feminine of G4552 (σάπφειρος); Sapphire, an Israelitess.

G4552.def: of Hebrew origin (H05601); a "sapphire" or lapis-lazuli gem.

G4553.def: apparently of Hebrew origin (H08276); a basket (as interwoven or wicker-work.

G4554.def: plural of uncertain derivation; Sardis, a place in Asia Minor.

G4555.def: from the same as G4556 (σάρδιος); sardine (G3037 (λίθος) being implied), i.e. a gem, so called.

G4556.def: properly, an adjective from an uncertain base; sardian (3037 being implied), i.e. (as noun) the gem so called.

G4557.def: from the base of G4556 (σάρδιος) and (the nail of a finger; hence the "onyx" stone); a "sardonyx", i.e. the gem so called.

G4558.def: of Hebrew origin (H06886); Sarepta (i.e. Tsarephath), a place in Palestine.

G4559.def: from G4561 (σάρξ); pertaining to flesh, i.e. (by extension) bodily, temporal, or (by implication) animal, unregenerate.

G4560.def: from G4561 (σάρξ); similar to flesh, i.e. (by analogy) soft.

G4561.def: probably from the base of G4563 (σαρόω); flesh (as stripped of the skin), i.e. (strictly) the meat of an animal (as food), or (by extension) the body (as opposed to the soul (or spirit), or as the symbol of what is external, or as the means of kindred), or (by implication) human nature (with its frailties (physically or morally) and passions), or (specially), a human being (as such)

G4562.def: of Hebrew origin (H08286); Saruch (i.e. Serug), a patriarch.

G4563.def: from a derivative of (to brush off; akin to G4951 (σύρω)); meaning a broom; to sweep.

G4564.def: of Hebrew origin (H08283); Sarra (i.e. Sarah), the wife of Abraham.

G4565.def: of Hebrew origin (H08289); Saron (i.e. Sharon), a district of Palestine.

G4566.def: of Hebrew origin (H07854); Satan, i.e. the devil.

G4567.def: of Chaldee origin corresponding to H04566 (with the definite affix); the accuser, i.e. the devil.

G4568.def: of Hebrew origin (H05429); a certain measure for things dry.

G4569.def: of Hebrew origin, the same as G4549 (Σαούλ); Saulus (i.e. Shaul), the Jewish name of Paul.

G4570.def: a prolonged form of an apparently primary verb; to extinguish (literally or figuratively)

G4571.def: accusative case singular of G4771 (σύ); thee.

G4572.def: genitive case from G4571 (σέ) and G846 (αὐτός), also dative case of the same, , and accusative case , likewise contracted , , and , respectively; of (with, to) thyself.

G4573.def: middle voice from a derivative of G4576 (σέβομαι); to venerate, i.e. adore.

G4574.def: from G4573 (σεβάζομαι); something adored, i.e. an object of worship (god, altar, etc)

G4575.def: from G4573 (σεβάζομαι); venerable (august), i.e. (as noun) a title of the Roman Emperor, or (as adjective) imperial.

G4576.def: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to revere, i.e. adore.

G4577.def: probably from G4951 (σύρω) through its congener (to fasten; akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι)); a chain (as binding or drawing)

G4578.def: from G4579 (σείω); a commotion, i.e. (of the air) a gale, (of the ground) an earthquake.

G4579.def: apparently a primary verb; to rock (vibrate, properly, sideways or to and fro), i.e. (generally) to agitate (in any direction; cause to tremble); figuratively, to throw into a tremor (of fear or concern)

G4580.def: of Latin origin; "second"; Secundus, a Christian.

G4581.def: from (Seleucus, a Syrian king); Seleuceia, a place in Syria.

G4582.def: from (brilliancy; probably akin to the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι), through the idea of attractiveness); the moon.

G4583.def: middle voice or passive from a presumed derivative of G4582 (σελήνη); to be moon-struck, i.e. crazy.

G4584.def: of Hebrew origin (H08096); Semei (i.e. Shimi), an Israelite.

G4585.def: probably of foreign origin; fine wheaten flour.

G4588.def: of Latin origin; Sergius, a Roman.

G4589.def: of Hebrew origin (H08352); Seth (i.e. Sheth), a patriarch.

G4590.def: of Hebrew origin (H08035); Sem (i.e. Shem), a patriarch.

G4591.def: from (a mark; of uncertain derivation); to indicate.

G4592.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of the base of G4591 (σημαίνω); an indication, especially ceremonially or supernaturally.

G4593.def: from G4592 (σημεῖον); to distinguish, i.e. mark (for avoidance)

G4594.def: neuter (as adverb) of a presumed compound of the article G3588 (ὁ) (t changed to s) and G2250 (ἡμέρα); on the (i.e. this) day (or night current or just passed); generally, now (i.e. at present, hitherto)

G4595.def: apparently a primary verb; to putrefy, i.e. (figuratively) perish.

G4596.def: from (an Indian tribe from whom silk was procured; hence the name of the silk-worm); Seric, i.e. silken (neuter as noun, a silky fabric)

G4597.def: apparently of Hebrew origin (H05580); a moth.

G4598.def: from G4597 (σής) and a derivative of G977 (βιβρώσκω); moth-eaten.

G4599.def: from (bodily vigor; probably akin to the base of G2476 (ἵστημι)); to strengthen, i.e. (figuratively) confirm (in spiritual knowledge and power)

G4600.def: of uncertain derivation; the jaw-bone, i.e. (by implication) the cheek or side of the face.

G4601.def: from G4602 (σιγή); to keep silent (transitively or intransitively)

G4602.def: apparently from (to hiss, i.e. hist or hush); silence.

G4605.def: of Hebrew origin (H06721); Sidon (i.e. Tsidon), a place in Palestine.

G4606.def: from G4605 (Σιδών); a Sidonian, i.e. inhabitant of Sidon.

G4607.def: of Latin origin; a dagger-man or assassin; a freebooter (Jewish fanatic outlawed by the Romans)

G4608.def: of Hebrew origin (H07941); an intoxicant, i.e. intensely fermented liquor.

G4609.def: contraction for G4610 (Σιλουανός); Silas, a Christian.

G4610.def: of Latin origin; "silvan"; Silvanus, a Christian.

G4611.def: of Hebrew origin (H07975); Siloam (i.e. Shiloach), a pool of Jerusalem.

G4612.def: of Latin origin; a semicinctium or half-girding, i.e. narrow covering (apron)

G4613.def: of Hebrew origin (H08095); Simon (i.e. Shimon), the name of nine Israelites.

G4614.def: of Hebrew origin (H05514); Sina (i.e. Sinai), a mountain in Arabia.

G4615.def: perhaps from (to hurt, i.e. sting); mustard (the plant)

G4616.def: of uncertain (perhaps foreign) origin; byssos, i.e. bleached linen (the cloth or a garment of it)

G4618.def: from a derivative of G4621 (σῖτος); grain-fed, i.e. fattened.

G4619.def: from a derivative of G4621 (σῖτος); grained, i.e. fatted.

G4620.def: from G4621 (σῖτος) and G3358 (μέτρον); a grain-measure, i.e. (by implication) ration (allowance of food)

G4621.def: of uncertain derivation; grain, especially wheat.

G4622.def: of Hebrew origin (H06726); Sion (i.e. Tsijon), a hill of Jerusalem; figuratively, the Church (militant or triumphant)

G4623.def: from (silence, i.e. a hush; properly, muteness, i.e. involuntary stillness, or inability to speak; and thus differing from G4602 (σιγή), which is rather a voluntary refusal or indisposition to speak, although the terms are often used synonymously); to be dumb (but not deaf also, like 2974 properly); figuratively, to be calm (as quiet water)

G4624.def: from G4625 (σκάνδαλον); to entrap, i.e. trip up (figuratively, stumble (transitively) or entice to sin, apostasy or displeasure)

G4625.def: ("scandal"); probably from a derivative of G2578 (κάμπτω); a trap-stick (bent sapling), i.e. snare (figuratively, cause of displeasure or sin)
G4625.kjv: occasion to fall (of stumbling), offence, thing that offends, stumblingblock

G4626.def: apparently a primary verb; to dig.

G4627.def: a "skiff" (as if dug out), or yawl (carried aboard a large vessel for landing)

G4628.def: apparently from (to parch; through the idea of leanness); the leg (as lank)

G4629.def: from a derivative of (a covering; perhaps akin to the base of G4649 (σκοπός) through the idea of noticeableness); clothing.

G4630.def: apparently of Latin origin; left-handed; Scevas (i.e. Scævus), an Israelite.

G4631.def: from G4632 (σκεῦος); furniture, i.e. spare tackle.

G4632.def: of uncertain affinity; a vessel, implement, equipment or apparatus (literally or figuratively [specially, a wife as contributing to the usefulness of the husband])

G4633.def: apparently akin to G4632 (σκεῦος) and G4639 (σκιά); a tent or cloth hut (literally or figuratively)

G4634.def: from G4636 (σκῆνος) and G4078 (πήγνυμι); the Festival of Tabernacles (so called from the custom of erecting booths for temporary homes)

G4635.def: from G4633 (σκηνή) and G4160 (ποιέω); a manufacturer of tents.

G4636.def: from G4633 (σκηνή); a hut or temporary residence, i.e. (figuratively) the human body (as the abode of the spirit)

G4637.def: from G4636 (σκῆνος); to tent or encamp, i.e. (figuratively) to occupy (as a mansion) or (specially), to reside (as God did in the Tabernacle of old, a symbol of protection and communion)

G4638.def: from G4637 (σκηνόω); an encampment, i.e. (figuratively) the Temple (as God's residence), the body (as a tenement for the soul)

G4639.def: apparently a primary word; "shade" or a shadow (literally or figuratively (darkness of error or an adumbration))

G4640.def: akin to (to skip); to jump, i.e. sympathetically move (as the quickening of a fetus)

G4641.def: feminine of a compound of G4642 (σκληρός) and G2588 (καρδία); hard-heartedness, i.e. (specially), destitution of (spiritual) perception.

G4642.def: from the base of G4628 (σκέλος); dry, i.e. hard or tough (figuratively, harsh, severe)

G4644.def: from G4642 (σκληρός) and G5137 (τράχηλος); hardnaped, i.e. (figuratively) obstinate.

G4645.def: from G4642 (σκληρός); to indurate, i.e. (figuratively) render stubborn.

G4646.def: from the base of G4628 (σκέλος); warped, i.e. winding; figuratively, perverse.

G4647.def: perhaps from the base of G4628 (σκέλος) and G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); withered at the front, i.e. a point or prickle (figuratively, a bodily annoyance or disability)

G4648.def: from G4649 (σκοπός); to take aim at (spy), i.e. (figuratively) regard.
G4648.kjv: consider, take heed, look at (on), mark

G4649.def: from (to peer about ("skeptic"); perhaps akin to G4626 (σκάπτω) through the idea of concealment; compare G4629 (σκέπασμα)); a watch (sentry or scout), i.e. (by implication) a goal.

G4650.def: apparently from the same as G4651 (σκορπίος) (through the idea of penetrating); to dissipate, i.e. (figuratively) put to flight, waste, be liberal.

G4651.def: probably from an obsolete (perhaps strengthened from the base of G4649 (σκοπός) and meaning to pierce); a "scorpion" (from its sting)

G4653.def: from G4655 (σκότος); dimness, obscurity (literally or figuratively)

G4654.def: from G4655 (σκότος); to obscure (literally or figuratively)

G4655.def: from the base of G4639 (σκιά); shadiness, i.e. obscurity (literally or figuratively)

G4656.def: from G4655 (σκότος); to obscure or blind (literally or figuratively)

G4657.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of G1519 (εἰς) and G2965 (κύων) and G906 (βάλλω); what is thrown to the dogs, i.e. refuse (ordure)

G4658.def: probably of foreign origin; a Scythene or Scythian, i.e. (by implication) a savage.

G4659.def: from (sullen) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); angry-visaged, i.e. gloomy or affecting a mournful appearance.

G4660.def: apparently a primary verb; to flay, i.e. (figuratively) to harass.

G4661.def: neuter from G4660 (σκύλλω); something stripped (as a hide), i.e. booty.

G4662.def: from G4663 (σκώληξ) and a derivative of G977 (βιβρώσκω); worm-eaten, i.e. diseased with maggots.

G4663.def: of uncertain derivation; a grub, maggot or earth-worm.

G4665.def: of uncertain derivation; the emerald or green gem so called.

G4667.def: the same as G4666 (σμύρνα); Smyrna, a place in Asia Minor.

G4669.def: from G4667 (Σμύρνα); to tincture with myrrh, i.e. embitter (as a narcotic)

G4670.def: plural of Hebrew origin (H05467); Sodoma (i.e. Sedom), a place in Palestine.

G4671.def: dative case of G4771 (σύ); to thee.

G4672.def: of Hebrew origin (H08010); Solomon (i.e. Shelomoh), the son of David.

G4673.def: probably akin to the base of G4987 (σωρεύω); a funereal receptacle (urn, coffin), i.e. (by analogy) a bier.

G4675.def: genitive case of G4771 (σύ); of thee, thy.
G4675.kjv: X home, thee, thine (own), thou, thy

G4676.def: of Latin origin; a sudarium (sweat-cloth), i.e. towel (for wiping the perspiration from the face, or binding the face of a corpse)

G4677.def: of Hebrew origin (H07799 feminine); lily; Susannah (i.e. Shoshannah), an Israelitess.

G4678.def: from G4680; wisdom (higher or lower, worldly or spiritual).

G4679.def: from G4680 (σοφός); to render wise; in a sinister acceptation, to form "sophisms", i.e. continue plausible error.

G4680.def: akin to (clear); wise (in a most general application)

G4681.def: probably of foreign origin; Spania, a region of Europe.

G4682.def: prolongation from (to grasp; apparently strengthened from G4685 (σπάω) through the idea of spasmodic contraction); to mangle, i.e. convluse with epilepsy.

G4683.def: from (a strip; from a derivative of the base of G4682 (σπαράσσω) meaning to strap or wrap with strips); to swathe (an infant after the Oriental custom)

G4686.def: of immediate Latin origin, but ultimately a derivative of G138 (αἱρέομαι) in the sense of its cognate G1507 (εἱλίσσω); a coil (spira, "spire"), i.e. (figuratively) a mass of men (a Roman military cohort; also (by analogy) a squad of Levitical janitors)

G4687.def: probably strengthened from G4685 (σπάω) (through the idea of extending); to scatter, i.e. sow (literally or figuratively)

G4688.def: of Latin origin; a speculator, i.e. military scout (spy or (by extension) life-guardsman)

G4689.def: apparently a primary verb; to pour out as a libation, i.e. (figuratively) to devote (one's life or blood, as a sacrifice) ("spend")

G4690.def: from G4687 (σπείρω); something sown, i.e. seed (including the male "sperm"); by implication, offspring; specially, a remnant (figuratively, as if kept over for planting)

G4691.def: from G4690 (σπέρμα) and G3004 (λέγω); a seed-picker (as the crow), i.e. (figuratively) a sponger, loafer (specially, a gossip or trifler in talk)

G4692.def: probably strengthened from G4228 (πούς); to "speed" ("study"), i.e. urge on (diligently or earnestly); by implication, to await eagerly.

G4693.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of (a grotto); a cavern; by implication, a hiding-place or resort.

G4694.def: of uncertain derivation; a ledge or reef of rock in the sea.

G4695.def: from G4696 (σπίλος); to stain or soil (literally or figuratively)

G4696.def: of uncertain derivation; a stain or blemish, i.e. (figuratively) defect, disgrace.

G4697.def: middle voice from G4698 (σπλάγχνον); to have the bowels yearn, i.e. (figuratively) feel sympathy, to pity.

G4698.def: probably strengthened from (the "spleen"); an intestine (plural); figuratively, pity or sympathy.

G4701.def: from G4687 (σπείρω); a sowing, i.e. (by implication) parentage.

G4702.def: from G4703 (σπόρος); sown, i.e. (neuter plural) a planted field.

G4703.def: from G4687 (σπείρω); a scattering (of seed), i.e. (concretely) seed (as sown)

G4704.def: from G4710 (σπουδή); to use speed, i.e. to make effort, be prompt or earnest.
G4704.kjv: do (give) diligence, be diligent (forward), endeavour, labour, study

G4706.def: neuter of G4707 (σπουδαιότερος) as adverb; more earnestly than others), i.e. very promptly.

G4707.def: comparative of G4705 (σπουδαῖος); more prompt, more earnest.

G4708.def: adverb from G4707 (σπουδαιότερος); more speedily, i.e. sooner than otherwise.

G4710.def: from G4692 (σπεύδω); "speed", i.e. (by implication) despatch, eagerness, earnestness.

G4711.def: from G4687 (σπείρω) (as woven); a hamper or lunch-receptacle.

G4712.def: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι) (as fixed); a stade or certain measure of distance; by implication, a stadium or race-course.

G4713.def: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι) (as stationary); a jar or earthen tank.

G4714.def: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); a standing (properly, the act), i.e. (by analogy) position (existence); by implication, a popular uprising; figuratively, controversy.

G4715.def: from the base of G2746 (καύχησις); a stander (standard of value), i.e. (specially), a stater or certain coin.

G4716.def: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); a stake or post (as set upright), i.e. (specially), a pole or cross (as an instrument of capital punishment); figuratively, exposure to death, i.e. self-denial; by implication, the atonement of Christ.

G4717.def: from G4716 (σταυρός); to impale on the cross; figuratively, to extinguish (subdue) passion or selfishness.

G4718.def: probably from the base of G4735 (στέφανος); a cluster of grapes (as if intertwined)

G4719.def: from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); a head of grain (as standing out from the stalk)

G4720.def: the same as G4719 (στάχυς); Stachys, a Christian.

G4721.def: strengthened from a primary (a "thatch" or "deck" of a building); a roof.

G4722.def: from G4721 (στέγη); to roof over, i.e. (figuratively) to cover with silence (endure patiently)

G4723.def: a contraction from G4731 (στερεός) (as stiff and unnatural); "sterile".

G4724.def: probably strengthened from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); properly, to set fast ("stall"), i.e. (figuratively) to repress (reflexively, abstain from associating with)

G4725.def: from the base of G4735 (στέφανος); a wreath for show.

G4727.def: from G4728 (στενός); to make (intransitively, be) in straits, i.e. (by implication) to sigh, murmur, pray inaudibly.

G4728.def: probably from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι); narrow (from obstacles standing close about)

G4729.def: from the same as G4730 (στενοχωρία); to hem in closely, i.e. (figuratively) cramp.

G4730.def: from a compound of G4728 (στενός) and G5561 (χώρα); narrowness of room, i.e. (figuratively) calamity.

G4731.def: from G2476 (ἵστημι); stiff, i.e. solid, stable (literally or figuratively)

G4732.def: from G4731 (στερεός); to solidify, i.e. confirm (literally or figuratively)

G4733.def: from G4732 (στερεόω); something established, i.e. (abstractly) confirmation (stability)

G4734.def: probably contraction for (crowned; from G4737 (στεφανόω)); Stephanas, a Christian.

G4735.def: from an apparently primary (to twine or wreathe); a chaplet (as a badge of royalty, a prize in the public games or a symbol of honor generally; but more conspicuous and elaborate than the simple fillet, G1238 (διάδημα)), literally or figuratively.

G4736.def: the same as G4735 (στέφανος); Stephanus, a Christian.

G4737.def: from G4735 (στέφανος); to adorn with an honorary wreath (literally or figuratively)

G4738.def: from G2476 (ἵστημι) (as standing prominently); the (entire external) bosom, i.e. chest.

G4739.def: from the perfect tense of G2476 (ἵστημι); to be stationary, i.e. (figuratively) to persevere.

G4741.def: from a presumed derivative of G2476 (ἵστημι) (like G4731 (στερεός)); to set fast, i.e. (literally) to turn resolutely in a certain direction, or (figuratively) to confirm.

G4742.def: from a primary (to "stick", i.e. prick); a mark incised or punched (for recognition of ownership), i.e. (figuratively) scar of service.

G4743.def: feminine of G4742 (στίγμα); a point of time, i.e. an instant.

G4744.def: apparently a primary verb; to gleam, i.e. flash intensely.

G4745.def: probably from G2476 (ἵστημι); a colonnade or interior piazza.

G4746.def: from a primary (to "step" or "stamp"); a spread (as if tramped flat) of loose materials for a couch, i.e. (by implication) a bough of a tree so employed.

G4747.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of the base of G4748 (στοιχέω); something orderly in arrangement, i.e. (by implication) a serial (basal, fundamental, initial) constituent (literally), proposition (figuratively)

G4748.def: from a derivative of (to range in regular line); to march in (military) rank (keep step), i.e. (figuratively) to conform to virtue and piety.

G4749.def: from G4724 (στέλλω); equipment, i.e. (specially), a "stole" or long-fitting gown (as a mark of dignity)

G4750.def: probably strengthened from a presumed derivative of the base of G5114 (τομώτερος); the mouth (as if a gash in the face); by implication, language (and its relations); figuratively, an opening (in the earth); specially, the front or edge (of a weapon)

G4751.def: from G4750 (στόμα); an orifice (the gullet), i.e. (specially), the "stomach".

G4752.def: from G4754 (στρατεύομαι); military service, i.e. (figuratively) the apostolic career (as one of hardship and danger)

G4753.def: from G4754 (στρατεύομαι); an armament, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops (more or less extensive or systematic)

G4754.def: middle voice from the base of G4756 (στρατιά); to serve in a military campaign; figuratively, to execute the apostolate (with its arduous duties and functions), to contend with carnal inclinations.

G4755.def: from the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and G71 (ἄγω) or G2233 (ἡγέομαι); a general, i.e. (by implication or analogy) a (military) governor (prætor), the chief (præfect) of the (Levitical) temple-wardens.

G4756.def: feminine of a derivative of (an army; from the base of G4766 (στρώννυμι), as encamped); camp-likeness, i.e. an army, i.e. (figuratively) the angels, the celestial luminaries.

G4757.def: from a presumed derivative of the same as G4756 (στρατιά); a camper-out, i.e. a (common) warrior (literally or figuratively)

G4758.def: from a compound of the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its original sense); to gather (or select) as a warrior, i.e. enlist in the army.

G4759.def: from G4760 (στρατόπεδον) and G757 (ἄρχω); a ruler of an army, i.e. (specially), a Prætorian præfect.

G4760.def: from the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and the same as G3977 (πεδινός); a camping-ground, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops.

G4761.def: from a derivative of G4762 (στρέφω); to wrench, i.e. (specially), to torture (by the rack), but only figuratively, to pervert.

G4762.def: strengthened from the base of G5157 (τροπή); to twist, i.e. turn quite around or reverse (literally or figuratively)
G4762.kjv: convert, turn (again, back again, self, self about)

G4763.def: from a presumed derivative of G4764 (στρῆνος); to be luxurious.

G4764.def: akin to G4731 (στερεός); a "straining", "strenuousness" or "strength", i.e. (figuratively) luxury (voluptuousness)

G4765.def: diminutive of (a sparrow); a little sparrow.

G4766.def: prolongation from a still simpler , (used only as an alternate in certain tenses) (probably akin to G4731 (στερεός) through the idea of positing); to "strew," i.e. spread (as a carpet or couch)

G4767.def: from a derivative of an obsolete apparently primary (to hate); hated, i.e. odious.

G4768.def: from the same as G4767 (στυγνητός); to render gloomy, i.e. (by implication) glower (be overcast with clouds, or sombreness of speech)

G4769.def: from (to stiffen; properly akin to the base of G2476 (ἵστημι)); a post ("style"), i.e. (figuratively) support.

G4770.def: from G4745 (στοά); a "Stoic" (as occupying a particular porch in Athens), i.e. adherent of a certin philosophy.

G4771.def: the personal pronoun of the second person singular; thou.

G4773.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1085 (γένος); a relative (by blood); by extension, a fellow countryman.

G4774.def: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G1097 (γινώσκω); fellow knowledge, i.e. concession.

G4775.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2521 (κάθημαι); to seat oneself in company with.

G4776.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2523 (καθίζω); to give (or take) a seat in company with.

G4777.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2553 (κακοπαθέω); to suffer hardship in company with.

G4778.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2558 (κακουχέω); to maltreat in company with, i.e. (passively) endure persecution together.

G4779.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2564 (καλέω); to convoke.

G4780.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2572 (καλύπτω); to conceal altogether.

G4781.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2578 (κάμπτω); to bend together, i.e. (figuratively) to afflict.

G4782.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2597 (καταβαίνω); to descend in company with.

G4783.def: from G4784 (συγκατατίθεμαι); a deposition (of sentiment) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) accord with.

G4784.def: middle from G4862 (σύν) and G2698 (κατατίθημι); to deposit (one's vote or opinion) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to accord with.

G4785.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G2596 (κατά) and G5585 (ψηφίζω); to count down in company with, i.e. enroll among.

G4786.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2767 (κεράννυμι); to commingle, i.e. (figuratively) to combine or assimilate.

G4787.def: from G4682 (σπαράσσω) and G2795 (κινέω); to move together, i.e. (specially), to excite as a mass (to sedition)

G4788.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2808 (κλείω); to shut together, i.e. include or (figuratively) embrace in a common subjection to.

G4789.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2818 (κληρονόμος); a co-heir, i.e. (by analogy) participant in common.

G4790.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2841 (κοινωνέω); to share in company with, i.e. co-participate in.
G4790.kjv: communicate (have fellowship) with, be partaker of

G4791.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2844 (κοινωνός); a co-participant.

G4792.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2865 (κομίζω); to convey together, i.e. collect or bear away in company with others.

G4793.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2919 (κρίνω); to judge of one thing in connection with another, i.e. combine (spiritual ideas with appropriate expressions) or collate (one person with another by way of contrast or resemblance)

G4794.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2955 (κύπτω); to stoop altogether, i.e. be completely overcome by.

G4795.def: from a comparative of G4862 (σύν) and (to light or happen; from the base of G2962 (κύριος)); concurrence, i.e. accident.

G4796.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5463 (χαίρω); to sympathize in gladness, congratulate.

G4797.def: from G4862 (σύν) and (to pour) or its alternate; to commingle promiscuously, i.e. (figuratively) to throw (an assembly) into disorder, to perplex (the mind)
G4797.kjv: confound, confuse, stir up, be in an uproar

G4798.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5530 (χράομαι); to use jointly, i.e. (by implication) to hold intercourse in common.

G4799.def: from G4797 (συγχέω); commixture, i.e. (figuratively) riotous disturbance.

G4800.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2198 (ζάω); to continue to live in common with, i.e. co-survive (literally or figuratively)

G4801.def: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2201 (ζεῦγος); to yoke together, i.e. (figuratively) conjoin (in marriage)

G4802.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2212 (ζητέω); to investigate jointly, i.e. discuss, controvert, cavil.

G4803.def: from G4802 (συζητέω); mutual questioning, i.e. discussion.

G4804.def: from G4802 (συζητέω); a disputant, i.e. sophist.

G4805.def: from G4801 (συζεύγνυμι); co-yoked, i.e. (figuratively) as noun, a colleague; probably rather as a proper name; Syzygus, a Christian.

G4806.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2227 (ζωοποιέω); to reanimate conjointly with (figuratively)

G4807.def: of Hebrew origin (H08256) in imitation of G4809 (συκομωραία); a sycamore-fig tree.

G4809.def: from G4810 (σῦκον) and (the mulberry); the "sycamore"-fig tree.

G4810.def: apparently a primary word; a fig.

G4811.def: from a compound of G4810 (σῦκον) and a derivative of G5316 (φαίνω); to be a fig-informer (reporter of the law forbidding the exportation of figs from Greece), "sycophant", i.e. (genitive and by extension) to defraud (exact unlawfully, extort)
G4811.kjv: accuse falsely, take by false accusation

G4812.def: from the base of G4813 (συλάω) and (the reduplicated form of) G71 (ἄγω); to lead away as booty, i.e. (figuratively) seduce.

G4813.def: from a derivative of (to strip; probably akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι); compare G4661 (σκῦλον)); to despoil.

G4814.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2980 (λαλέω); to talk together, i.e. converse.

G4815.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to clasp, i.e. seize (arrest, capture); specially, to conceive (literally or figuratively); by implication, to aid.

G4816.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3004 (λέγω) in its original sense; to collect.

G4817.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3049 (λογίζομαι); to reckon together (with oneself), i.e. deliberate.

G4818.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3076 (λυπέω); to afflict jointly, i.e. (passive) sorrow at (on account of) someone.

G4819.def: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to walk (figuratively, transpire) together, i.e. concur (take place)

G4820.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G906 (βάλλω); to combine, i.e. (in speaking) to converse, consult, dispute, (mentally) to consider, (by implication) to aid, (personally) to join, attack.
G4820.kjv: confer, encounter, help, make, meet with, ponder

G4821.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G936 (βασιλεύω); to be co-regent (figuratively)

G4822.def: from G4862 (σύν) and (to force; causative (by reduplication) of the base of G939 (βάσις)); to drive together, i.e. unite (in association or affection), (mentally) to infer, show, teach.
G4822.kjv: compact, assuredly gather, intrust, knit together, prove

G4823.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1011 (βουλεύω); to give (or take) advice jointly, i.e. recommend, deliberate or determine.

G4824.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of G4825 (σύμβουλος); advisement; specially, a deliberative body, i.e. the provincial assessors or lay-court.

G4825.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1012 (βουλή); a consultor, i.e. adviser.

G4826.def: from the same as G4613 (Σίμων); Symeon (i.e. Shimon), the name of five Israelites.

G4827.def: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3129 (μανθάνω); a co-learner (of Christianity)

G4828.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3140 (μαρτυρέω); to testify jointly, i.e. corroborate by (concurrent) evidence.

G4829.def: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and G3307 (μερίζω); to share jointly, i.e. participate in.

G4830.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3353 (μέτοχος); a co-participant.

G4831.def: from a presumed compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3401 (μιμέομαι); a co-imitator, i.e. fellow votary.

G4832.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3444 (μορφή); jointly formed, i.e. (figuratively) similar.

G4833.def: from G4832 (σύμμορφος); to render like, i.e. (figuratively) to assimilate.

G4834.def: from G4835 (συμπαθής); to feel "sympathy" with, i.e. (by implication) to commiserate.
G4834.kjv: have compassion, be touched with a feeling of

G4835.def: from G4841 (συμπάσχω); having a fellow-feeling ("sympathetic"), i.e. (by implication) mutually commiserative.

G4836.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3854 (παραγίνομαι); to be present together, i.e. to convene; by implication, to appear in aid.

G4837.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3870 (παρακαλέω); to console jointly.

G4838.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3880 (παραλαμβάνω); to take along in company.

G4839.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3887 (παραμένω); to remain in company, i.e. still live.

G4840.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3918 (πάρειμι); to be at hand together, i.e. now present.

G4841.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3958 (πάσχω) (including its alternate); to experience pain jointly or of the same kind (specially, persecution; to "sympathize")

G4842.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3992 (πέμπω); to despatch in company.

G4843.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G4012 (περί) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take by enclosing altogether, i.e. earnestly throw the arms about one.

G4844.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4095 (πίνω); to partake a beverage in company.

G4845.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4137 (πληρόω); to implenish completely, i.e. (of space) to swamp (a boat), or (of time) to accomplish (passive, be complete)

G4846.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4155 (πνίγω); to strangle completely, i.e. (literally) to drown, or (figuratively) to crowd.

G4847.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4177 (πολίτης); a native of the same town, i.e. (figuratively) co-religionist (fellow-Christian)

G4848.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4198 (πορεύομαι); to journey together; by implication, to assemble.

G4849.def: neuter of a derivative of the alternate of G4844 (συμπίνω); a drinking-party ("symposium"), i.e. (by extension) a room of guests.

G4850.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4245 (πρεσβύτερος); a co-presbyter.

G4851.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5342 (φέρω) (including its alternate); to bear together (contribute), i.e. (literally) to collect, or (figuratively) to conduce; especially (neuter participle as a noun) advantage.
G4851.kjv: be better for, bring together, be expedient (for), be good, (be) profit(-able for)

G4852.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5346 (φημί); to say jointly, i.e. assent to.

G4853.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G5443 (φυλή); a co-tribesman, i.e. native of the same country.

G4854.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G5453 (φύω); grown along with (connate), i.e. (figuratively) closely united to.

G4855.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5453 (φύω); passive, to grow jointly.

G4856.def: from G4859 (σύμφωνος); to be harmonious, i.e. (figuratively) to accord (be suitable, concur) or stipulate (by compact)

G4858.def: from G4859 (σύμφωνος); unison of sound ("symphony"), i.e. a concert of instruments (harmonious note)

G4859.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5456 (φωνή); sounding together (alike), i.e. (figuratively) accordant (neuter as noun, agreement)

G4860.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5585 (ψηφίζω); to compute jointly.

G4861.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5590 (ψυχή); co-spirited, i.e. similar in sentiment.

G4862.def: a primary preposition denoting union; with or together (but much closer than G3326 (μετά) or G3844 (παρά)), i.e. by association, companionship, process, resemblance, possession, instrumentality, addition, etc.

G4863.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead together, i.e. collect or convene; specially, to entertain (hospitably)
G4863.kjv: + accompany, assemble (selves, together), bestow, come together, gather (selves together, up, together), lead into, resort, take in

G4864.def: from (the reduplicated form of) G4863 (συνάγω); an assemblage of persons; specially, a Jewish "synagogue" (the meeting or the place); by analogy, a Christian church.

G4865.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G75 (ἀγωνίζομαι); to struggle in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to be a partner (assistant)

G4866.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G118 (ἀθλέω); to wrestle in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to seek jointly.

G4867.def: from G4862 (σύν) and (to hoard); to convene.

G4868.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G142 (αἴρω); to make up together, i.e. (figuratively) to compute (an account)

G4869.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G164 (αἰχμάλωτος); a co-captive.

G4870.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G190 (ἀκολουθέω); to accompany.

G4871.def: from G4862 (σύν) and (to throng); to accumulate, i.e. convene.

G4872.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G305 (ἀναβαίνω); to ascend in company with.

G4873.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G345 (ἀνακεῖμαι); to recline in company with (at a meal)
G4873.kjv: sit (down, at the table, together) with (at meat)

G4874.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G303 (ἀνά) and G3396 (μίγνυμι); to mix up together, i.e. (figurative) associate with.

G4875.def: middle from G4862 (σύν) and G373 (ἀναπαύω); to recruit oneself in company with.

G4876.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί); to meet with; figuratively, to occur.

G4878.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G482 (ἀντιλαμβάνομαι); to take hold of opposite together, i.e. co-operate (assist)

G4879.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G520 (ἀπάγω); to take off together, i.e. transport with (seduce, passively, yield)

G4880.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G599 (ἀποθνήσκω); to decease (literally) in company with, or (figuratively), similarly to.

G4881.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G622 (ἀπόλλυμι); to destroy (middle voice or passively, be slain) in company with.

G4882.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G649 (ἀποστέλλω); to despatch (on an errand) in company with.

G4883.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of G719 (ἁρμός) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its original sense of laying); to render close-jointed together, i.e. organize compactly.

G4884.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G726 (ἁρπάζω); to snatch together, i.e. seize.

G4885.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G837 (αὐξάνω); to increase (grow up) together.

G4886.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1199 (δεσμόν); a joint tie, i.e. ligament, (figuratively) uniting principle, control.

G4887.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1210 (δέω); to bind with, i.e. (passively) be a fellow-prisoner (figuratively)

G4888.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1392 (δοξάζω); to exalt to dignity in company (i.e. similarly) with.

G4889.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1401 (δοῦλος); a co-slave, i.e. servitor or ministrant of the same master (human or divine)

G4890.def: from (the alternate of) G4936 (συντρέχω); a running together, i.e. (riotous) concourse.

G4891.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1453 (ἐγείρω); to rouse (from death) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to revivify (spirtually) in resemblance to.

G4892.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of a compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος); a joint session, i.e. (specially), the Jewish Sanhedrin; by analogy, a subordinate tribunal.

G4893.def: from a prolonged form of G4894 (συνείδω); co-perception, i.e. moral consciousness.

G4894.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1492 (εἴδω); to see completely; used (like its primary) only in two past tenses, respectively meaning to understand or become aware, and to be conscious or (clandestinely) informed of.
G4894.kjv: consider, know, be privy, be ware of

G4895.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1510 (εἰμί) (including its various inflections); to be in company with, i.e. present at the time.

G4896.def: from G4862 (σύν) and (to go); to assemble.

G4897.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1525 (εἰσέρχομαι); to enter in company with.
G4897.kjv: go in with, go with into

G4898.def: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G1553 (ἐκδημέω); a co-absentee from home, i.e. fellow-traveller.

G4899.def: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G1586 (ἐκλέγομαι); chosen in company with, i.e. co-elect (fellow Christian)

G4900.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1643 (ἐλαύνω); to drive together, i.e. (figuratively) exhort (to reconciliation)

G4901.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G1957 (ἐπιμαρτυρέω); to testify further jointly, i.e. unite in adding evidence.

G4902.def: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and a primary (to follow); to attend (travel) in company with.

G4903.def: from G4904 (συνεργός); to be a fellow-worker, i.e. co-operate.

G4904.def: from a presumed compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον); a co-laborer, i.e. coadjutor.
G4904.kjv: companion in labour, (fellow-)helper(-labourer, -worker), labourer together with, workfellow

G4905.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2064 (ἔρχομαι); to convene, depart in company with, associate with, or (specially), cohabit (conjugally)
G4905.kjv: accompany, assemble (with), come (together), come (company, go) with, resort

G4906.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2068 (ἐσθίω) (including its alternate); to take food in company with.

G4907.def: from G4920 (συνίημι); a mental putting together, i.e. intelligence or (concretely) the intellect.

G4908.def: from G4920 (συνίημι); mentally put (or putting) together, i.e. sagacious.

G4909.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2106 (εὐδοκέω); to think well of in common, i.e. assent to, feel gratified with.
G4909.kjv: allow, assent, be pleased, have pleasure

G4910.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a presumed compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G2192 (ἔχω) (meaning to be in good condition, i.e. (by implication) to fare well, or feast); to entertain sumptuously in company with, i.e. (middle voice or passive) to revel together.

G4911.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2186 (ἐφίστημι); to stand up together, i.e. to resist (or assault) jointly.

G4912.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold together, i.e. to compress (the ears, with a crowd or siege) or arrest (a prisoner); figuratively, to compel, perplex, afflict, preoccupy.
G4912.kjv: constrain, hold, keep in, press, lie sick of, stop, be in a strait, straiten, be taken with, throng

G4913.def: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2237 (ἡδονή); to rejoice in with oneself, i.e. feel satisfaction concerning.

G4914.def: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G2239 (ἦθος); mutual habituation, i.e. usage.

G4915.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G2244 (ἡλικία); a co-aged person, i.e. alike in years.

G4916.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2290 (θάπτω); to inter in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to assimilate spiritually (to Christ by a sepulture as to sin)

G4917.def: from G4862 (σύν) and thlao (to crush); to dash together, i.e. shatter.

G4918.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2346 (θλίβω); to compress, i.e. crowd on all sides.

G4919.def: from G4862 (σύν) and (to crumble); to crush together, i.e. (figuratively) to dispirit.

G4920.def: from G4862 (σύν) and (to send); to put together, i.e. (mentally) to comprehend; by implication, to act piously.

G4921.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G2476 (ἵστημι) (including its collateral forms); to set together, i.e. (by implication) to introduce (favorably), or (figuratively) to exhibit; intransitively, to stand near, or (figuratively) to constitute.

G4922.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3593 (ὁδεύω); to travel in company with.

G4923.def: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3598 (ὁδός) ("synod"); companionship on a journey, i.e. (by implication), a caravan.

G4924.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3611 (οἰκέω); to reside together (as a family)

G4925.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3618 (οἰκοδομέω); to construct, i.e. (passively) to compose (in company with other Christians, figuratively)

G4926.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G3656 (ὁμιλέω); to converse mutually.

G4927.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of the base of 3674 and the base of G3725 (ὅριον); to border together, i.e. adjoin.

G4929.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5021 (τάσσω); to arrange jointly, i.e. (figuratively) to direct.

G4930.def: from G4931 (συντελέω); entire completion, i.e. consummation (of a dispensation)

G4931.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5055 (τελέω); to complete entirely; generally, to execute (literally or figuratively)

G4932.def: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G5114 (τομώτερος); to contract by cutting, i.e. (figuratively) do concisely (speedily)

G4933.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5083 (τηρέω); to keep closely together, i.e. (by implication) to conserve (from ruin); mentally, to remember (and obey)

G4934.def: middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place jointly, i.e. (figuratively) to consent (bargain, stipulate), concur.

G4935.def: adverb from a derivative of G4932 (συντέμνω); concisely (briefly)

G4936.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5143 (τρέχω) (including its alternate); to rush together (hastily assemble) or headlong (figuratively)

G4937.def: from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G5147 (τρίβος); to crush completely, i.e. to shatter (literally or figuratively)
G4937.kjv: break (in pieces), broken to shivers (+ -hearted), bruise

G4938.def: from G4937 (συντρίβω); concussion or utter fracture (properly, concretely), i.e. complete ruin.

G4939.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5162 (τροφός) (in a passive sense); a fellow-nursling, i.e. comrade.

G4940.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5177 (τυγχάνω); to chance together, i.e. meet with (reach)

G4941.def: from G4940 (συντυγχάνω); an accident; Syntyche, a Christian female.

G4942.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5271 (ὑποκρίνομαι); to act hypocritically in concert with.

G4943.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον); to be a co-auxiliary, i.e. assist.

G4944.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G5605 (ὠδίνω); to have (parturition) pangs in company (concert, simultaneously) with, i.e. (figuratively) to sympathize (in expectation of relief from suffering)

G4945.def: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3660 (ὀμνύω); a swearing together, i.e. (by implication) a plot.

G4946.def: plural of uncertain derivation; Syracuse, the capital of Sicily.

G4947.def: probably of Hebrew origin (H06865); Syria (i.e. Tsyria or Tyre), a region of Asia.

G4948.def: from the same as G4947 (Συρία); a Syran (i.e. probably Tyrian), a native of Syria.

G4949.def: feminine of a compound of G4948 (Σύρος) and the same as G5403 (Φοινίκη); a Syro-phœnician woman, i.e. a female native of Phœnicia in Syria.

G4950.def: from G4951 (σύρω); a shoal (from the sand drawn thither by the waves), i.e. the Syrtis Major or great bay on the north coast of Africa.

G4951.def: probably akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι); to trail.

G4952.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4682 (σπαράσσω); to rend completely, i.e. (by analogy) to convulse violently.

G4953.def: neuter of a compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G4591 (σημαίνω); a sign in common, i.e. preconcerted signal.

G4954.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4983 (σῶμα); of a joint body, i.e. (figuratively) a fellow-member of the Christian community.

G4955.def: from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G4714 (στάσις); a fellow-insurgent.

G4956.def: from a derivative of G4921 (συνιστάω); introductory, i.e. recommendatory.

G4957.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4717 (σταυρόω); to impale in company with (literally or figuratively)

G4958.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4724 (στέλλω); to send (draw) together, i.e. enwrap (enshroud a corpse for burial), contract (an interval)

G4959.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4727 (στενάζω); to moan jointly, i.e. (figuratively) experience a common calamity.

G4960.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4748 (στοιχέω); to file together (as soldiers in ranks), i.e. (figuratively) to correspond to.

G4961.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4757 (στρατιώτης); a co-campaigner, i.e. (figuratively) an associate in Christian toil.

G4962.def: from G4862 (σύν) and G4762 (στρέφω); to twist together, i.e. collect (a bundle, a crowd)

G4963.def: from G4962 (συστρέφω); a twisting together, i.e. (figuratively) a secret coalition, riotous crowd.

G4964.def: from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G4976 (σχῆμα); to fashion alike, i.e. conform to the same pattern (figuratively)
G4964.kjv: conform to, fashion self according to

G4965.def: of Hebrew origin (H07941); Sychar (i.e. Shekar), a place in Palestine.

G4966.def: of Hebrew origin (H07927); Sychem (i.e. Shekem), the name of a Canaanite and of a place in Palestine.

G4967.def: from G4969 (σφάζω); butchery (of animals for food or sacrifice, or (figuratively) of men (destruction))

G4968.def: neuter of a derivative of G4967 (σφαγή); a victim (in sacrifice)

G4969.def: a primary verb; to butcher (especially an animal for food or in sacrifice) or (generally) to slaughter, or (specially), to maim (violently)

G4970.def: neuter plural of (violent; of uncertain derivation) as adverb; vehemently, i.e. in a high degree, much.

G4971.def: adverb from the same as G4970 (σφόδρα); very much.

G4972.def: from G4973 (σφραγίς); to stamp (with a signet or private mark) for security or preservation (literally or figuratively); by implication, to keep secret, to attest.
G4972.kjv: (set a, set to) seal up, stop

G4973.def: probably strengthened from G5420 (φράσσω); a signet (as fencing in or protecting from misappropriation); by implication, the stamp impressed (as a mark of privacy, or genuineness), literally or figuratively.

G4974.def: neuter of a presumed derivative probably of the same as (a ball, "sphere"; compare the feminine , a hammer); the ankle (as globular)

G4975.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of the alternate of G2192 (ἔχω) as adverb; nigh, i.e. nearly.

G4976.def: from the alternate of G2192 (ἔχω); a figure (as a mode or circumstance), i.e. (by implication) external condition.

G4977.def: apparently a primary verb; to split or sever (literally or figuratively)
G4977.kjv: break, divide, open, rend, make a rent

G4978.def: from G4977 (σχίζω); a split or gap ("schism"), literally or figuratively.

G4979.def: diminutive of (a rush or flag-plant; of uncertain derivation); a rushlet, i.e. grass-withe or tie (generally)

G4980.def: from G4981 (σχολή); to take a holiday, i.e. be at leisure for (by implication, devote oneself wholly to); figuratively, to be vacant (of a house)

G4981.def: probably feminine of a presumed derivative of the alternate of G2192 (ἔχω); properly, loitering (as a withholding of oneself from work) or leisure, i.e. (by implication) a "school" (as vacation from physical employment)

G4982.def: from a primary σῶς (contraction for obsolete σάος, 'safe'); to save, i.e., deliver or protect (literally or figuratively).

G4983.def: from G4982 (σώζω); the body (as a sound whole), used in a very wide application, literally or figuratively.

G4985.def: adverb from G4984 (σωματικός); corporeally or physically.

G4986.def: from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and G3962 (πατήρ); of a safe father; Sopatrus, a Christian.

G4987.def: from another form of G4673 (σορός); to pile up (literally or figuratively)

G4988.def: from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and that of G4599 (σθενόω); of safe strength; Sosthenes, a Christian.

G4989.def: prolongation for G4986 (Σώπατρος); Sosipatrus, a Christian.

G4990.def: from G4982 (σώζω); a deliverer, i.e. God or Christ.

G4991.def: feminine of a derivative of G4990 as (properly, abstract) noun; rescue or safety (physically or morally).

G4992.def: neuter of the same as G4991 (σωτηρία) as (properly, concretely) noun; defender or (by implication) defence.

G4993.def: from G4998 (σώφρων); to be of sound mind, i.e. sane, (figuratively) moderate.
G4993.kjv: be in right mind, be sober (minded), soberly

G4994.def: from G4998 (σώφρων); to make of sound mind, i.e. (figuratively) to discipline or correct.

G4996.def: adverb from G4998 (σώφρων); with sound mind, i.e. moderately.

G4997.def: from G4998 (σώφρων); soundness of mind, i.e. (literally) sanity or (figuratively) self-control.

G4998.def: from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and that of G5424 (φρήν); safe (sound) in mind, i.e. self-controlled (moderate as to opinion or passion)

G4999.def: plural of Latin origin; huts or wooden-walled buildings; Tabernæ.

G5000.def: of Chaldee origin (compare H06646); the gazelle; Tabitha (i.e. Tabjetha), a Christian female.

G5001.def: from G5021 (τάσσω); something orderly in arrangement (a troop), i.e. (figuratively) a series or succession.

G5002.def: from G5021 (τάσσω); arranged, i.e. appointed or stated.

G5003.def: from G5005 (ταλαίπωρος); to be wretched, i.e. realize one's own misery.

G5005.def: from the base of G5007 (τάλαντον) and a derivative of the base of G3984 (πεῖρα); enduring trial, i.e. miserable.

G5007.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of the original form of (to bear; equivalent to G5342 (φέρω)); a balance (as supporting weights), i.e. (by implication) a certain weight (and thence a coin or rather sum of money) or "talent".

G5008.def: of Chaldee origin (compare H02924); the fresh, i.e. young girl; talitha (O maiden)

G5009.def: neuter contraction of a presumed derivative of (a dispenser or distributor; akin to , to cut); a dispensary or magazine, i.e. a chamber on the ground-floor or interior of an Oriental house (generally used for storage or privacy, a spot for retirement)

G5010.def: from G5021 (τάσσω); regular arrangement, i.e. (in time) fixed succession (of rank or character), official dignity.

G5011.def: of uncertain derivation; depressed, i.e. (figuratively) humiliated (in circumstances or disposition)
G5011.kjv: base, cast down, humble, of low degree (estate), lowly

G5012.def: from a compound of G5011 (ταπεινός) and the base of G5424 (φρήν); humiliation of mind, i.e. modesty.
G5012.kjv: humbleness of mind, humility (of mind, loneliness (of mind)

G5013.def: from G5011 (ταπεινός); to depress; figuratively, to humiliate (in condition or heart)

G5014.def: from G5013 (ταπεινόω); depression (in rank or feeling)
G5014.kjv: humiliation, be made low, low estate, vile

G5015.def: of uncertain affinity; to stir or agitate (roil water)

G5016.def: feminine from G5015 (ταράσσω); disturbance, i.e. (of water) roiling, or (of a mob) sedition.

G5017.def: masculine from G5015 (ταράσσω); a disturbance, i.e. (popular) tumult.

G5018.def: from G5019 (Ταρσός); a Tarsean, i.e. native of Tarsus.

G5019.def: perhaps the same as (a flat basket); Tarsus, a place in Asia Minor.

G5020.def: from (the deepest abyss of Hades); to incarcerate in eternal torment.

G5021.def: a prolonged form of a primary verb (which latter appears only in certain tenses); to arrange in an orderly manner, i.e. assign or dispose (to a certain position or lot)

G5022.def: apparently a primary word (compare H08450, "steer"); a bullock.

G5023.def: nominative or accusative case neuter plural of G3778 (οὗτος); these things.
G5023.kjv: + afterward, follow, + hereafter, X him, the same, so, such, that, then, these, they, this, those, thus

G5024.def: neuter plural of G3588 (ὁ) and G846 (αὐτός) as adverb; in the same way.

G5025.def: dative case and accusative case feminine plural respectively of G3778 (οὗτος); (to or with or by, etc.) these.

G5026.def: dative case, accusative case and genitive case respectively of the feminine singular of G3778 (οὗτος); (towards or of) this.
G5026.kjv: her, + hereof, it, that, + thereby, the (same), this (same)

G5028.def: masculine from G2290 (θάπτω); a grave (the place of interment)

G5029.def: as if neuter plural of G5036 (ταχύς) (adverbially); shortly, i.e. (figuratively) possibly.

G5030.def: adverb from G5036 (ταχύς); briefly, i.e. (in time) speedily, or (in manner) rapidly.

G5032.def: neuter singular of the comparative of G5036 (ταχύς) (as adverb); more swiftly, i.e. (in manner) more rapidly, or (in time) more speedily.

G5033.def: neuter plural of the superlative of G5036 (ταχύς) (as adverb); most quickly, i.e. (with G5613 (ὡς) prefixed) as soon as possible.

G5034.def: from the same as G5036 (ταχύς); a brief space (of time), i.e. (with G1722 (ἐν) prefixed) in haste.

G5035.def: neuter singular of G5036 (ταχύς) (as adverb); shortly, i.e. without delay, soon, or (by surprise) suddenly, or (by implication, of ease) readily.

G5036.def: of uncertain affinity; fleet, i.e. (figuratively) prompt or ready.

G5037.def: a primary particle (enclitic) of connection or addition; both or also (properly, as correlation of G2532 (καί))
G5037.kjv: also, and, both, even, then, whether

G5038.def: akin to the base of G5088 (τίκτω); a wall (as formative of a house)

G5039.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of (a goal or fixed limit); a token (as defining a fact), i.e. criterion of certainty.

G5040.def: diminutive of G5043 (τέκνον); an infant, i.e. (plural figuratively) darlings (Christian converts)

G5041.def: from a compound of G5043 (τέκνον) and the base of G1096 (γίνομαι); to be a child-bearer, i.e. parent (mother)

G5042.def: from the same as G5041 (τεκνογονέω); childbirth (parentage), i.e. (by implication) maternity (the performance of maternal duties)

G5043.def: from the base of G5098 (τιμωρία); a child (as produced)

G5044.def: from a compound of G5043 (τέκνον) and G5142 (τρέφω); to be a childrearer, i.e. fulfil the duties of a female parent.

G5045.def: from the base of G5098 (τιμωρία); an artificer (as producer of fabrics), i.e. (specially), a craftsman in wood.

G5046.def: from G5056 (τέλος); complete (in various applications of labor, growth, mental and moral character, etc.); neuter (as noun, with G3588 (ὁ)) completeness.

G5047.def: from G5046 (τέλειος); (the state) completeness (mentally or morally)

G5048.def: from G5046 (τέλειος); to complete, i.e. (literally) accomplish, or (figuratively) consummate (in character)

G5049.def: adverb from G5046 (τέλειος); completely, i.e. (of hope) without wavering.

G5050.def: from G5448 (φυσιόω); (the act) completion, i.e. (of prophecy) verification, or (of expiation) absolution.

G5051.def: from G5048 (τελειόω); a completer, i.e. consummater.

G5052.def: from a compound of G5056 (τέλος) and G5342 (φέρω); to be a bearer to completion (maturity), i.e. to ripen fruit (figuratively)

G5053.def: from a presumed derivative of G5055 (τελέω); to finish life (by implication, of G979 (βίος)), i.e. expire (demise)

G5055.def: from G5056 (τέλος); to end, i.e. complete, execute, conclude, discharge (a debt)
G5055.kjv: accomplish, make an end, expire, fill up, finish, go over, pay, perform

G5056.def: from a primary (to set out for a definite point or goal); properly, the point aimed at as a limit, i.e. (by implication) the conclusion of an act or state (termination (literally, figuratively or indefinitely), result (immediate, ultimate or prophetic), purpose); specially, an impost or levy (as paid)

G5057.def: from G5056 (τέλος) and G5608 (ὠνέομαι); a tax-farmer, i.e. collector of public revenue.

G5058.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of G5057 (τελώνης); a tax-gatherer's place of business.

G5059.def: of uncertain affinity; a prodigy or omen.

G5060.def: of Latin origin; third; Tertius, a Christian.

G5061.def: of uncertain derivation; Tertullus, a Roman.

G5063.def: from G5062 (τεσσαράκοντα) and G2094 (ἔτος); of forty years of age.

G5065.def: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G2532 (καί) and G1182 (δέκατος); fourteenth.

G5066.def: from G5064 (τέσσαρες); pertaining to the fourth day.

G5068.def: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G1137 (γωνία); four-cornered, i.e. square.

G5069.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of (a tetrad; from G5064 (τέσσαρες)); a quaternion or squad (picket) of four Roman soldiers.

G5070.def: from the multiplicative adverb of G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G5507 (χίλιοι); four times a thousand.

G5071.def: plural from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G1540 (ἑκατόν); four hundred.

G5072.def: neuter of a compound of G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G3376 (μήν); a four months' space.

G5073.def: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and a derivative of the base of G4118 (πλεῖστος); quadruple.

G5074.def: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G4228 (πούς); a quadruped.

G5075.def: from G5076 (τετράρχης); to be a tetrarch.

G5076.def: from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G757 (ἄρχω); the ruler of a fourth part of a country ("tetrarch")

G5078.def: from the base of G5088 (τίκτω); art (as productive), i.e. (specially), a trade, or (generally) skill.

G5079.def: from G5078 (τέχνη); an artisan; figuratively, a founder (Creator)

G5080.def: apparently a primary verb; to liquefy.

G5081.def: adverb from a compound of a derivative of G5056 (τέλος) and G827 (αὐγή); in a far-shining manner, i.e. plainly.

G5082.def: from a compound of G3588 (ὁ) with G2245 (ἡλίκος) and G3778 (οὗτος); such as this, i.e. (in (figurative) magnitude) so vast.

G5083.def: from (a watch; perhaps akin to G2334 (θεωρέω)); to guard (from loss or injury, properly, by keeping the eye upon; and thus differing from G5442 (φυλάσσω), which is properly to prevent escaping; and from G2892 (κουστωδία), which implies a fortress or full military lines of apparatus), i.e. to note (a prophecy; figuratively, to fulfil a command); by implication, to detain (in custody; figuratively, to maintain); by extension, to withhold (for personal ends; figuratively, to keep unmarried); by extension, to withhold (for personal ends; figuratively, to keep unmarried)
G5083.kjv: hold fast, keep(- er), (pre-, re-)serve, watch

G5084.def: from G5083 (τηρέω); a watching, i.e. (figuratively) observance, or (concretely) a prison.

G5085.def: from G5086 (Τιβέριος); Tiberias, the name of a town and a lake in Palestine.

G5086.def: of Latin origin; probably pertaining to the river Tiberis or Tiber; Tiberius, a Roman emperor.

G5087.def: a prolonged form of a primary (which is used only as alternate in certain tenses); to place (in the widest application, literally and figuratively; properly, in a passive or horizontal posture, and thus different from G2476 (ἵστημι), which properly denotes an upright and active position, while G2749 (κεῖμαι) is properly reflexive and utterly prostrate)
G5087.kjv: + advise, appoint, bow, commit, conceive, give, X kneel down, lay (aside, down, up), make, ordain, purpose, put, set (forth), settle, sink down

G5088.def: a strengthened form of a primary (which is used only as alternate in certain tenses); to produce (from seed, as a mother, a plant, the earth, etc.), literally or figuratively.
G5088.kjv: bear, be born, bring forth, be delivered, be in travail

G5089.def: perhaps akin to the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι), and thus to G4951 (σύρω); to pull off.

G5090.def: probably of Chaldee origin (compare H02931); Timæus (i.e. Timay), an Israelite.

G5091.def: from G5093 (τίμιος); to prize, i.e. fix a valuation upon; by implication, to revere.

G5092.def: from G5099 (τίνω); a value, i.e. money paid, or (concretely and collectively) valuables; by analogy, esteem (especially of the highest degree), or the dignity itself.

G5093.def: from G5092 (τιμή); valuable, i.e. (objectively) costly, or (subjectively) honored, esteemed, or (figuratively) beloved.
G5093.kjv: dear, honourable, (more, most) precious, had in reputation

G5094.def: from G5093 (τίμιος); expensiveness, i.e. (by implication) magnificence.

G5095.def: from G5092 (τιμή) and G2316 (θεός); dear to God; Timotheus, a Christian.

G5096.def: from G5092 (τιμή); valuable; Timon, a Christian.

G5097.def: from a comparative of G5092 (τιμή) and ouros (a guard); properly, to protect one's honor, i.e. to avenge (inflict a penalty)

G5098.def: from G5097 (τιμωρέω); vindication, i.e. (by implication) a penalty.

G5099.def: strengthened for a primary (which is only used as an alternate in certain tenses); to pay a price, i.e. as a penalty.

G5100.def: an enclitic indefinite pronoun; some or any person or object.
G5100.kjv: a (kind of), any (man, thing, thing at all), certain (thing), divers, he (every) man, one (X thing), ought, + partly, some (man, -body, - thing, -what), (+ that no-)thing, what(-soever), X wherewith, whom(-soever), whose(-soever)

G5101.def: probably emphatic of G5100 (τὶς); an interrogative pronoun, who, which or what (in direct or indirect questions)
G5101.kjv: every man, how (much), + no(-ne, thing), what (manner, thing), where (-by, -fore, -of, -unto, - with, -withal), whether, which, who(-m, -se), why

G5102.def: of Latin origin; a titulus or "title" (placard)

G5103.def: of Latin origin but uncertain significance; Titus, a Christian.

G5104.def: probably for the dative case of G3588 (ὁ); an enclitic particle of asseveration by way of contrast; in sooth.
G5104.kjv: (used only with other particles in the comparative, as G2544 (καίτοιγε), G3305 (μέντοι), G5105 (τοιγαροῦν), G5106 (τοίνυν), etc

G5105.def: from G5104 (τοί) and G1063 (γάρ) and G3767 (οὖν); truly for then, i.e. consequently.

G5106.def: from G5104 (τοί) and G3568 (νῦν); truly now, i.e. accordingly.

G5107.def: (including the other inflections); from a derivative of 5104 and G1161 (δέ); such-like then, i.e. so great.

G5108.def: (including the other inflections); from G5104 (τοί) and G3778 (οὗτος); truly this, i.e. of this sort (to denote character or individuality)

G5109.def: another form of G5038 (τεῖχος); a wall.

G5110.def: from the base of G5088 (τίκτω); interest on money loaned (as a produce)

G5111.def: from (boldness; probably itself from the base of G5056 (τέλος) through the idea of extreme conduct); to venture (objectively or in act; while G2292 (θαῤῥέω) is rather subjective or in feeling); by implication, to be courageous.

G5112.def: neuter of the compound of a derivative of the base of 5111 (as adverb); more daringly, i.e. with greater confidence than otherwise.

G5114.def: comparative of a derivative of the primary (to cut; more comprehensive or decisive than G2875 (κόπτω), as if by a single stroke; whereas that implies repeated blows, like hacking); more keen.

G5115.def: from the base of G5088 (τίκτω); a bow (apparently as the simplest fabric)

G5116.def: neuter of a presumed derivative (alternately) of (a "topaz"; of uncertain origin); a gem, probably the chrysolite.

G5117.def: apparently a primary word; a spot (general in space, but limited by occupancy; whereas G5561 (χώρα) is a large but participle locality), i.e. location (as a position, home, tract, etc.); figuratively, condition, opportunity; specially, a scabbard.
G5117.kjv: coast, licence, place, X plain, quarter, + rock, room, where

G5118.def: from (so much; apparently from G3588 (ὁ) and G3739 (ὅς)) and G3778 (οὗτος) (including its variations); so vast as this, i.e. such (in quantity, amount, number of space)
G5118.kjv: as large, so great (long, many, much), these many

G5119.def: from (the neuter of) G3588 (ὁ) and G3753 (ὅτε); the when, i.e. at the time that (of the past or future, also in consecution)

G5120.def: properly, the genitive case of G3588 (ὁ); sometimes used for G5127 (τούτου); of this person.

G5121.def: contraction for the neuter of G3588 (ὁ) and G1726 (ἐναντίον); on the contrary.

G5122.def: contraction for the neuter of G3588 (ὁ) and G3686 (ὄνομα); the name (is)

G5123.def: contraction for G5124 (τοῦτο) and G2076 (ἐστί); that is.

G5124.def: neuter singular nominative or accusative case of G3778 (οὗτος); that thing.
G5124.kjv: here (-unto), it, partly, self(-same), so, that (intent), the same, there(-fore, -unto), this, thus, where(-fore)

G5125.def: dative case plural masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος); to (for, in, with or by) these (persons or things)
G5125.kjv: such, them, there(-in, -with), these, this, those

G5126.def: accusative case singular masculine of G3778 (οὗτος); this (person, as objective of verb or preposition)

G5127.def: genitive case singular masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος); of (from or concerning) this (person or thing)
G5127.kjv: here(-by), him, it, + such manner of, that, thence(-forth), thereabout, this, thus

G5128.def: accusative case plural masculine of G3778 (οὗτος); these (persons, as objective of verb or preposition)

G5129.def: dative case singular masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος); to (in, with or by) this (person or thing)
G5129.kjv: here(-by, -in), him, one, the same, there(-in), this

G5130.def: genitive case plural masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος); of (from or concerning) these (persons or things)
G5130.kjv: such, their, these (things), they, this sort, those

G5131.def: from the base of G5176 (τρώγω); a he-goat (as a gnawer)

G5132.def: probably contracted from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G3979 (πεζῇ); a table or stool (as being four-legged), usually for food (figuratively, a meal); also a counter for money (figuratively, a broker's office for loans at interest)

G5133.def: from G5132 (τράπεζα); a money-broker or banker.

G5134.def: from the base of (to wound; akin to the base of G2352 (θραύω), G5147 (τρίβος), G5149 (τρίζω), etc.); a wound.

G5135.def: from G5134 (τραῦμα); to inflict a wound.

G5136.def: from G5137 (τράχηλος); to seize by the throat or neck, i.e. to expose the gullet of a victim for killing (generally, to lay bare)

G5137.def: probably from G5143 (τρέχω) (through the idea of mobility); the throat (neck), i.e. (figuratively) life.

G5138.def: perhaps strengthened from the base of G4486 (ῥήγνυμι) (as if jagged by rents); uneven, rocky (reefy)

G5139.def: from a derivative of G5138 (τραχύς); rough district; Trachonitis, a region of Syria.

G5141.def: strengthened from a primary (to "dread", "terrify"); to "tremble" or fear.

G5142.def: a primary verb (properly, ; but perhaps strengthened from the base of G5157 (τροπή) through the idea of convolution); properly, to stiffen, i.e. fatten (by implication, to cherish (with food, etc.), pamper, rear)

G5143.def: apparently a primary verb (properly, ; compare G2359 (θρίξ)); which uses (the base of G1408 (δρόμος)) as alternate in certain tenses; to run or walk hastily (literally or figuratively)

G5145.def: plural from G5140 (τρεῖς) and G1540 (ἑκατόν); three hundred.

G5146.def: from G5140 (τρεῖς) and G956 (βέλος); properly, a crow-foot (three-pronged obstruction in war), i.e. (by analogy) a thorny plant (caltrop)

G5147.def: from (to "rub"; akin to , , and the base of G5131 (τράγος), G5134 (τραῦμα)); a rut or worn track.

G5148.def: from a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G2094 (ἔτος); a three years' period (triennium)

G5149.def: apparently a primary verb; to creak (squeak), i.e. (by analogy) to grate the teeth (in frenzy)

G5150.def: neuter of a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G3376 (μήν) as noun; a three months' space.

G5152.def: neuter of a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G4721 (στέγη) as noun; a third roof (story)

G5153.def: from G5151 (τρίς) and G5507 (χίλιοι); three times a thousand.

G5154.def: ordinal from G5140 (τρεῖς); third; neuter (as noun) a third part, or (as adverb) a (or the) third time, thirdly.

G5155.def: from G2359 (θρίξ); hairy, i.e. made of hair (mohair)

G5156.def: from G5141 (τρέμω); a "trembling", i.e. quaking with fear.

G5157.def: from an apparently primary to turn; a turn ("trope"), i.e. revolution (figuratively, variation)

G5158.def: from the same as G5157 (τροπή); a turn, i.e. (by implication) mode or style (especially with preposition or relative prefix as adverb, like); figuratively, deportment or character.
G5158.kjv: (even) as, conversation, (+ like) manner, (+ by any) means, way

G5159.def: from G5158 (τρόπος) and G5409 (φορέω); to endure one's habits.

G5160.def: from G5142 (τρέφω); nourishment (literally or figuratively); by implication, rations (wages)

G5161.def: from G5160 (τροφή); nutritive; Trophimus, a Christian.

G5162.def: from G5142 (τρέφω); a nourisher, i.e. nurse.

G5163.def: from G5164 (τροχός); a track (as a wheel-rut), i.e. (figuratively) a course of conduct.

G5164.def: from G5143 (τρέχω); a wheel (as a runner), i.e. (figuratively) a circuit of physical effects.

G5165.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of uncertain affinity; a bowl.

G5166.def: from a derivative of (to dry) meaning ripe fruit (as if dry); to collect the vintage.

G5167.def: from (to murmur; akin to G5149 (τρίζω), but denoting a duller sound); a turtle-dove (as cooing)

G5168.def: from a derivative of (to wear away; akin to the base of G5134 (τραῦμα), G5147 (τρίβος) and G5176 (τρώγω)); an orifice, i.e. needle's eye.

G5169.def: from a derivative of the base of G5168 (τρυμαλιά); an aperture, i.e. a needle's eye.

G5170.def: from G5172 (τρυφή); luxurious; Tryphæna, a Christian woman.

G5171.def: from G5172 (τρυφή); to indulge in luxury.

G5172.def: from (to break up or (figuratively) enfeeble, especially the mind and body by indulgence); effeminacy, i.e. luxury or debauchery.

G5173.def: from G5172 (τρυφή); luxuriating; Tryphosa, a Christian female.

G5174.def: from (a Trojan); the Troad (or plain of Troy), i.e. Troas, a place in Asia Minor.

G5175.def: of uncertain derivation; Trogyllium, a place in Asia Minor.
