G5176.def: probably strengthened from a collateral form of the base of G5134 (τραῦμα) and G5147 (τρίβος) through the idea of corrosion or wear; or perhaps rather of a base of G5167 (τρυγών) and G5149 (τρίζω) through the idea of a craunching sound; to gnaw or chew, i.e. (generally) to eat.

G5177.def: probably for an obsolete (for which the middle voice of another alternate (to make ready or bring to pass) is used in certain tenses; akin to the base of G5088 (τίκτω) through the idea of effecting; properly, to affect; or (specially), to hit or light upon (as a mark to be reached), i.e. (transitively) to attain or secure an object or end, or (intransitively) to happen (as if meeting with); but in the latter application only impersonal (with G1487 (εἰ)), i.e. perchance; or (present participle) as adjective, usual (as if commonly met with, with G3756 (οὐ), extraordinary), neuter (as adverb) perhaps; or (with another verb) as adverb, by accident (as it were)
G5177.kjv: be, chance, enjoy, little, obtain, X refresh…self, + special

G5178.def: from a derivative of G5180 (τύπτω) (meaning a drum, "tympanum"); to stretch on an instrument of torture resembling a drum, and thus beat to death.

G5179.def: from G5180 (τύπτω); a die (as struck), i.e. (by implication) a stamp or scar; by analogy, a shape, i.e. a statue, (figuratively) style or resemblance; specially, a sampler ("type"), i.e. a model (for imitation) or instance (for warning)
G5179.kjv: en-(ex-)ample, fashion, figure, form, manner, pattern, print

G5180.def: a primary verb (in a strengthened form); to "thump", i.e. cudgel or pummel (properly, with a stick or bastinado), but in any case by repeated blows; thus differing from G3817 (παίω) and G3960 (πατάσσω), which denote a (usually single) blow with the hand or any instrument, or G4141 (πλήσσω) with the fist (or a hammer), or G4474 (ῥαπίζω) with the palm; as well as from G5177 (τυγχάνω), an accidental collision); by implication, to punish; figuratively, to offend (the conscience)

G5181.def: a provincial form of the derivative of the base of G2962 (κύριος); a "tyrant"; Tyrannus, an Ephesian.

G5182.def: from (Latin turba, a crowd; akin to G2351 (θόρυβος)); to make "turbid", i.e. disturb.

G5183.def: from G5184 (Τύρος); a Tyrian, i.e. inhabitant of Tyrus.

G5184.def: of Hebrew origin (H06865): Tyrus (i.e. Tsor), a place in Palestine.

G5185.def: from, G5187 (τυφόω); opaque (as if smoky), i.e. (by analogy) blind (physically or mentally)

G5186.def: from G5185 (τυφλός); to make blind, i.e. (figuratively) to obscure.

G5187.def: from a derivative of G5188 (τύφω); to envelop with smoke, i.e. (figuratively) to inflate with self-conceit.
G5187.kjv: high-minded, be lifted up with pride, be proud

G5188.def: apparently a primary verb; to make a smoke, i.e. slowly consume without flame.

G5189.def: from a derivative of G5188 (τύφω); stormy (as if smoky)

G5190.def: from a derivative of G5177 (τυγχάνω); fortuitous, i.e. fortunate; Tychicus, a Christian.

G5191.def: from G5192 (ὑάκινθος); "hyacinthine" or "jacinthine", i.e. deep blue.

G5192.def: of uncertain derivation; the "hyacinth" or "jacinth", i.e. some gem of a deep blue color, probably the zirkon.

G5194.def: perhaps from the same as G5205 (ὑετός) (as being transparent like rain); glass.

G5195.def: from G5196 (ὕβρις); to exercise violence, i.e. abuse.

G5196.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ); insolence (as over-bearing), i.e. insult, injury.

G5197.def: from G5195 (ὑβρίζω); an insulter, i.e. maltreater.

G5198.def: from G5199 (ὑγιής); to have sound health, i.e. be well (in body); figuratively, to be uncorrupt (true in doctrine)
G5198.kjv: be in health, (be safe and) sound, (be) whole(-some)

G5199.def: from the base of G837 (αὐξάνω); healthy, i.e. well (in body); figuratively, true (in doctrine)

G5200.def: from the base of G5205 (ὑετός); wet (as if with rain), i.e. (by implication) sappy (fresh)

G5201.def: from G5204 (ὕδωρ); a water-jar, i.e. receptacle for family supply.

G5202.def: from a compound of G5204 (ὕδωρ) and a derivative of G4095 (πίνω); to be a water-drinker, i.e. to abstain from vinous beverages.

G5203.def: from a compound of G5204 (ὕδωρ) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι) (as if looking watery); to be "dropsical".

G5204.def: from the base of G5205 (ὑετός); water (as if rainy) literally or figuratively.

G5205.def: from a primary (to rain); rain, especially a shower.

G5206.def: from a presumed compound of G5207 (υἱός) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι); the placing as a son, i.e. adoption (figuratively, Christian sonship in respect to God)

G5207.def: apparently a primary word; a 'son' (sometimes of animals), used very widely of immediate, remote or figuratively, kinship.

G5208.def: perhaps akin to G3586 (ξύλον); a forest, i.e. (by implication) fuel.

G5209.def: accusative case of G5210 (ὑμεῖς); you (as the objective of a verb or preposition)

G5210.def: irregular plural of G4771 (σύ); you (as subjective of verb)

G5211.def: from (the god of weddings); "hymenæal"; Hymenæus, an opponent of Christianity.

G5212.def: from G5210 (ὑμεῖς); yours, i.e. pertaining to you.

G5213.def: irregular dative case of G5210 (ὑμεῖς); to (with or by) you.

G5214.def: from G5215 (ὕμνος); to hymn, i.e. sing a religious ode; by implication, to celebrate (God) in song.

G5215.def: apparently from a simpler (obsolete) form of (to celebrate; probably akin to G103 (ᾄδω); compare H05667); a "hymn" or religious ode (one of the Psalms)

G5216.def: genitive case of G5210 (ὑμεῖς); of (from or concerning) you.

G5217.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G71 (ἄγω); to lead (oneself) under, i.e. withdraw or retire (as if sinking out of sight), literally or figuratively.

G5218.def: from G5219 (ὑπακούω); attentive hearkening, i.e. (by implication) compliance or submission.

G5219.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G191 (ἀκούω); to hear under (as a subordinate), i.e. to listen attentively; by implication, to heed or conform to a command or authority.

G5220.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G435 (ἀνήρ); in subjection under a man, i.e. a married woman.

G5221.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί); to go opposite (meet) under (quietly), i.e. to encounter, fall in with.

G5222.def: from G5221 (ὑπαντάω); an encounter or concurrence (with G1519 (εἰς) for infinitive, in order to fall in with)

G5223.def: from G5225 (ὑπάρχω); existency or proprietorship, i.e. (concretely) property, wealth.

G5224.def: neuter plural of present participle active of G5225 (ὑπάρχω) as noun; things extant or in hand, i.e. property or possessions.
G5224.kjv: goods, that which one has, things which (one) possesseth, substance, that hast

G5225.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G756 (ἄρχομαι); to begin under (quietly), i.e. come into existence (be present or at hand); expletively, to exist (as copula or subordinate to an adjective, participle, adverb or preposition, or as an auxiliary to a principal (verb)

G5226.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and (to yield, be "weak"); to surrender.

G5227.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1727 (ἐναντίος); under (covertly) contrary to, i.e. opposed or (as noun) an opponent.

G5228.def: a primary preposition; "over", i.e. (with the genitive case) of place, above, beyond, across, or causal, for the sake of, instead, regarding; with the accusative case superior to, more than.
G5228.kjv: (+ exceeding, abundantly) above, in (on) behalf of, beyond, by, + very chiefest, concerning, exceeding (above, -ly), for, + very highly, more (than), of, over, on the part of, for sake of, in stead, than, to(-ward), very

G5229.def: middle voice from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G142 (αἴρω); to raise oneself over, i.e. (figuratively) to become haughty.
G5229.kjv: exalt self, be exalted above measure

G5230.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the base of G188 (ἀκμήν); beyond the "acme", i.e. figuratively (of a daughter) past the bloom (prime) of youth.

G5231.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G507 (ἄνω); above upward, i.e. greatly higher (in place or rank)

G5232.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G837 (αὐξάνω); to increase above ordinary degree.

G5233.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the base of G939 (βάσις); to transcend, i.e. (figuratively) to overreach.

G5234.def: adverb from present participle active of G5235 (ὑπερβάλλω); excessively.

G5235.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw beyod the usual mark, i.e. (figuratively) to surpass (only active participle supereminent)

G5236.def: from G5235 (ὑπερβάλλω); a throwing beyond others, i.e. (figuratively) supereminence; adverbially (with G1519 (εἰς) or G2596 (κατά)) pre- eminently.
G5236.kjv: abundance, (far more) exceeding, excellency, more excellent, beyond (out of) measure

G5237.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G1492 (εἴδω); to overlook, i.e. not punish.

G5238.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the neuter plural of G1565 (ἐκεῖνος); above those parts, i.e. still farther.

G5239.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G1614 (ἐκτείνω); to extend inordinately.

G5240.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the alternate form of G1632 (ἐκχέω); to pour out over, i.e. (passively) to overflow.

G5241.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G1793 (ἐντυγχάνω); to intercede in behalf of.

G5242.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold oneself above, i.e. (figuratively) to excel; participle (as adjective, or neuter as noun) superior, superiority.

G5244.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G5316 (φαίνω); appearing above others (conspicuous), i.e. (figuratively) haughty.

G5245.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G3528 (νικάω); to vanquish beyond, i.e. gain a decisive victory.

G5246.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G3591 (ὄγκος); bulging over, i.e. (figuratively) insolent.

G5247.def: from G5242 (ὑπερέχω); prominence, i.e. (figuratively) superiority (in rank or character)

G5248.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G4052 (περισσεύω); to super-abound.

G5249.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G4057 (περισσῶς); superabundantly, i.e. exceedingly.

G5250.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G4121 (πλεονάζω); to superabound.

G5251.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G5312 (ὑψόω); to elevate above others, i.e. raise to the highest position.

G5252.def: from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and G5426 (φρονέω); to esteem oneself overmuch, i.e. be vain or arrogant.

G5253.def: neuter of a derivative of G5228 (ὑπέρ); a higher part of the house, i.e. apartment in the third story.

G5254.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2192 (ἔχω); to hold oneself under, i.e. endure with patience.

G5255.def: from G5219 (ὑπακούω); attentively listening, i.e. (by implication) submissive.

G5256.def: from G5257 (ὑπηρέτης); to be a subordinate, i.e. (by implication) subserve.

G5257.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of (to row); an under-oarsman, i.e. (generally) subordinate (assistant, sexton, constable)

G5258.def: from an obsolete primary (perhaps akin to G5259 (ὑπό) through the idea of subsilience); sleep, i.e. (figuratively) spiritual torpor.

G5259.def: a primary preposition; under, i.e. (with the genitive case) of place (beneath), or with verbs (the agency or means, through); (with the accusative case) of place (whither (underneath) or where (below) or time (when (at))
G5259.kjv: among, by, from, in, of, under, with

G5260.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G906 (βάλλω); to throw in stealthily, i.e. introduce by collusion.

G5261.def: from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G1125 (γράφω); an underwriting, i.e. copy for imitation (figuratively)

G5262.def: from G5263 (ὑποδείκνυμι); an exhibit for imitation or warning (figuratively, specimen, adumbration)

G5263.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1166 (δεικνύω); to exhibit under the eyes, i.e. (figuratively) to exemplify (instruct, admonish)

G5264.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1209 (δέχομαι); to admit under one's roof, i.e. entertain hospitably.

G5265.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1210 (δέω); to bind under one's feet, i.e. put on shoes or sandals.

G5266.def: from G5265 (ὑποδέω); something bound under the feet, i.e. a shoe or sandal.

G5267.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G1349 (δίκη); under sentence, i.e. (by implication) condemned.

G5268.def: neuter of a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G2218 (ζυγός); an animal under the yoke (draught-beast), i.e. (specially), a donkey.

G5269.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2224 (ζώννυμι); to gird under, i.e. frap (a vessel with cables across the keel, sides and deck)

G5270.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2736 (κάτω); down under, i.e. beneath.

G5271.def: middle voice from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2919 (κρίνω); to decide (speak or act) under a false part, i.e. (figuratively) dissemble (pretend)

G5272.def: from G5271 (ὑποκρίνομαι); acting under a feigned part, i.e. (figuratively) deceit ("hypocrisy")

G5273.def: from G5271 (ὑποκρίνομαι); an actor under an assumed character (stage-player), i.e. (figuratively) a dissembler ("hypocrite".

G5274.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G2983 (λαμβάνω); to take from below, i.e. carry upward; figuratively, to take up, i.e. continue a discourse or topic; mentally, to assume (presume)

G5275.def: from G5295 (ὑποτρέχω) and G3007 (λείπω); to leave under (behind), i.e. (passively) to remain (survive)

G5276.def: neuter of a presumed compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G3025 (ληνός); vessel or receptacle under the press, i.e. lower wine-vat.

G5277.def: a prolonged form for G5275 (ὑπολείπω); to leave behind, i.e. bequeath.

G5278.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G3306 (μένω); to stay under (behind), i.e. remain; figuratively, to undergo, i.e. bear (trials), have fortitude, persevere.
G5278.kjv: abide, endure, (take) patient(-ly), suffer, tarry behind

G5279.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G3403 (μιμνήσκω); to remind quietly, i.e. suggest to the (middle voice, one's own) memory.
G5279.kjv: put in mind, remember, bring to (put in) remembrance

G5280.def: from G5279 (ὑπομιμνήσκω); a reminding or (reflexively) recollection.

G5281.def: from G5278 (ὑπομένω); cheerful (or hopeful) endurance, constancy.

G5282.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G3539 (νοιέω); to think under (privately), i.e. to surmise or conjecture.

G5284.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4126 (πλέω); to sail under the lee of.

G5285.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4154 (πνέω); to breathe gently, i.e. breeze.

G5286.def: neuter of a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G4228 (πούς); something under the feet, i.e. a foot-rest (figuratively)

G5287.def: from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G2476 (ἵστημι); a setting under (support), i.e. (figuratively) concretely, essence, or abstractly, assurance (objectively or subjectively)

G5288.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4724 (στέλλω); to withhold under (out of sight), i.e. (reflexively) to cower or shrink, (figuratively) to conceal (reserve)

G5289.def: from G5288 (ὑποστέλλω); shrinkage (timidity), i.e. (by implication) apostasy.

G5290.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4762 (στρέφω); to turn under (behind), i.e. to return (literally or figuratively)
G5290.kjv: come again, return (again, back again), turn back (again)

G5291.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G4766 (στρώννυμι); to strew underneath (the feet as a carpet)

G5293.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5021 (τάσσω); to subordinate; reflexively, to obey.
G5293.kjv: be under obedience (obedient), put under, subdue unto, (be, make) subject (to, unto), be (put) in subjection (to, under), submit self unto

G5294.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5087 (τίθημι); to place underneath, i.e. (figuratively) to hazard, (reflexively) to suggest.

G5295.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5143 (τρέχω) (including its alternate); to run under, i.e. (specially), to sail past.

G5296.def: from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G5179 (τύπος); typification under (after), i.e. (concretely) a sketch (figuratively) for imitation.

G5297.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5342 (φέρω); to bear from underneath, i.e. (figuratively) to undergo hardship.

G5298.def: from G5259 (ὑπό) and G5562 (χωρέω); to vacate down, i.e. retire quietly.

G5299.def: from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι); to hit under the eye (buffet or disable an antagonist as a pugilist), i.e. (figuratively) to tease or annoy (into compliance), subdue (one's passions)

G5300.def: apparently a primary word; a hog ("swine")

G5302.def: from G5306 (ὕστερος); to be later, i.e. (by implication) to be inferior; generally, to fall short (be deficient)
G5302.kjv: come behind (short), be destitute, fail, lack, suffer need, (be in) want, be the worse

G5303.def: from G5302 (ὑστερέω); a deficit; specially, poverty.
G5303.kjv: that which is behind, (that which was) lack(-ing), penury, want

G5304.def: from G5302 (ὑστερέω); a falling short, i.e. (specially), penury.

G5305.def: neuter of G5306 (ὕστερος) as adverb; more lately, i.e. eventually.

G5306.def: comparative from G5259 (ὑπό) (in the sense of behind); later.

G5307.def: from to weave; woven, i.e. (perhaps) knitted.

G5308.def: from G5311 (ὕψος); lofty (in place or character)

G5309.def: from a compound of G5308 (ὑψηλός) and G5424 (φρήν); to be lofty in mind, i.e. arrogant.

G5310.def: superlative from the base of G5311 (ὕψος); highest, i.e. (masculine singular) the Supreme (God), or (neuter plural) the heavens.

G5311.def: from a derivative of G5228 (ὑπέρ); elevation, i.e. (abstractly) altitude, (specially), the sky, or (figuratively) dignity.

G5312.def: from G5311 (ὕψος); to elevate (literally or figuratively)

G5313.def: from G5312 (ὑψόω); an elevated place or thing, i.e. (abstractly) altitude, or (by implication) a barrier (figuratively)

G5315.def: a primary verb (used as an alternate of G2068 (ἐσθίω) in certain tenses); to eat (literally or figuratively)

G5316.def: prolongation for the base of G5457 (φῶς); to lighten (shine), i.e. show (transitive or intransitive, literal or figurative)
G5316.kjv: appear, seem, be seen, shine, X think

G5317.def: of Hebrew origin (H06389); Phalek (i.e. Peleg), a patriarch.

G5318.def: from G5316 (φαίνω); shining, i.e. apparent (literally or figuratively); neuter (as adverb) publicly, externally.
G5318.kjv: abroad, + appear, known, manifest, open (+ -ly), outward (+ -ly)

G5319.def: from G5318 (φανερός); to render apparent (literally or figuratively)

G5320.def: adverb from G5318 (φανερός); plainly, i.e. clearly or publicly.

G5321.def: from G5319 (φανερόω); exhibition, i.e. (figuratively) expression, (by extension) a bestowment.

G5322.def: from G5316 (φαίνω); a lightener, i.e. light; lantern.

G5323.def: of Hebrew origin (H06439); Phanuel (i.e. Penuel), an Israelite.

G5324.def: from a derivative of G5316 (φαίνω); to make apparent, i.e. (passively) to appear (neuter participle as noun, a spectacle)

G5325.def: from a derivative of G5324 (φαντάζω); (properly abstract) a (vain) show ("fantasy")

G5326.def: from G5324 (φαντάζω); (properly concrete) a (mere) show ("phantasm"), i.e. spectre.

G5327.def: properly, strengthened from the base of G4008 (πέραν) or rather of G4486 (ῥήγνυμι); a gap or chasm, i.e. ravine (winter-torrent)

G5328.def: of foreign origin (H06547); Pharao (i.e. Pharoh), an Egyptian king.

G5329.def: of Hebrew origin (H06557); Phares (i.e. Perets), an Israelite.

G5330.def: of Hebrew origin (compare H06567); a separatist, i.e. exclusively religious; a Pharisean, i.e. Jewish sectary.

G5331.def: from G5332 (φαρμακεύς); medication ("pharmacy"), i.e. (by extension) magic (literally or figuratively)

G5332.def: from (a drug, i.e. spell-giving potion); a druggist ("pharmacist") or poisoner, i.e. (by extension) a magician.

G5334.def: from G5346 (φημί) (not the same as "phase", which is from G5316 (φαίνω)); a saying, i.e. report.

G5335.def: prolongation from the same as G5346 (φημί); to assert.

G5337.def: apparently a primary word; "foul" or "flawy", i.e. (figuratively) wicked.

G5338.def: probably akin to the base of G5457 (φῶς) (compare G5350 (φθέγγομαι)); brilliancy.

G5339.def: of uncertain affinity; to be chary of, i.e. (subjectively) to abstain or (objectively) to treat leniently.

G5340.def: adverb from participle of G5339 (φείδομαι); abstemiously, i.e. stingily.

G5341.def: by transposition for a derivative probably of G5316 (φαίνω) (as showing outside the other garments); a mantle (surtout)

G5342.def: a primary verb (for which other, and apparently not cognate ones are used in certain tenses only; namely, ; and ; to "bear" or carry (in a very wide application, literally and figuratively, as follows)
G5342.kjv: be, bear, bring (forth), carry, come, + let her drive, be driven, endure, go on, lay, lead, move, reach, rushing, uphold

G5343.def: apparently a primary verb; to run away (literally or figuratively); by implication, to shun; by analogy, to vanish.

G5344.def: of Latin origin; happy; Phelix (i.e. Felix), a Roman.

G5345.def: from G5346 (φημί); a saying, i.e. rumor ("fame")

G5346.def: properly, the same as the base of G5457 (φῶς) and G5316 (φαίνω); to show or make known one's thoughts, i.e. speak or say.

G5347.def: of Latin derivation; festal; Phestus (i.e. Festus), a Roman.

G5348.def: apparently a primary verb; to be beforehand, i.e. anticipate or precede; by extension, to have arrived at.

G5349.def: from G5351 (φθείρω); decayed, i.e. (by implication) perishable.

G5350.def: probably akin to G5338 (φέγγος) and thus to G5346 (φημί); to utter a clear sound, i.e. (generally) to proclaim.

G5351.def: probably strengthened from (to pine or waste); properly, to shrivel or wither, i.e. to spoil (by any process) or (generally) to ruin (especially figuratively, by moral influences, to deprave)

G5352.def: from derivative of (to wane; akin to the base of G5351 (φθείρω)) and G3703 (ὀπώρα) (meaning late autumn); autumnal (as stripped of leaves)

G5353.def: from G5350 (φθέγγομαι); utterance, i.e. a musical note (vocal or instrumental)

G5354.def: from G5355 (φθόνος); to be jealous of.

G5355.def: probably akin to the base of G5351 (φθείρω); ill-will (as detraction), i.e. jealousy (spite)

G5356.def: from G5351 (φθείρω); decay, i.e. ruin (spontaneous or inflicted, literally or figuratively)

G5357.def: of uncertain affinity; a broad shallow cup ("phial")

G5358.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G18 (ἀγαθός); fond to good, i.e. a promoter of virtue.

G5359.def: from (the same as G5361 (φιλάδελφος)), a king of Pergamos; Philadelphia, a place in Asia Minor.

G5360.kjv: brotherly love (kindness), love of the brethren

G5361.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G80 (ἀδελφός); fond of brethren, i.e. fraternal.

G5362.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G435 (ἀνήρ); fond of man, i.e. affectionate as a wife.

G5363.def: from the same as G5364 (φιλανθρώπως); fondness of mankind, i.e. benevolence ("philanthropy")

G5364.def: adverb from a compound of G5384 (φίλος) and G444 (ἄνθρωπος); fondly to man ("philanthropically"), i.e. humanely.

G5366.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G696 (ἄργυρος); fond of silver (money), i.e. avaricious.

G5367.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G846 (αὐτός); fond of self, i.e. selfish.

G5368.gloss: to love (as a friend), to kiss
G5368.def: from G5384; to be a friend to (fond of an individual or an object), i.e., have affection for.

G5369.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G2237 (ἡδονή); fond of pleasure, i.e. voluptuous.

G5371.def: from G5368 (φιλέω); friendly; Philemon, a Christian.

G5372.def: from G5368 (φιλέω); amiable; Philetus, an opposer of Christianity.

G5374.def: from G5375 (Φίλιπποι); a Philippesian (Philippian), i.e. native of Philippi.

G5375.def: plural of G5376 (Φίλιππος); Philippi, a place in Macedonia.

G5376.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G2462 (ἵππος); fond of horses; Philippus, the name of four Israelites.

G5377.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G2316 (θεός); fond of God, i.e. pious.

G5378.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G3056 (λόγος); fond of words, i.e. talkative (argumentative, learned, "philological"); Philologus, a Christian.

G5379.def: from G5380 (φιλόνεικος); quarrelsomeness, i.e. a dispute.

G5380.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and (a quarrel; probably akin to G3534 (νῖκος)); fond of strife, i.e. disputatious.

G5382.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G3581 (ξένος); fond of guests, i.e. hospitable.

G5383.def: from a compound of G5384 (φίλος) and G4413 (πρῶτος); to be fond of being first, i.e. ambitious of distinction.

G5384.def: properly, dear, i.e., a friend; actively, fond, i.e., friendly (still as a noun, an associate, neighbor, etc.).

G5386.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G4680 (σοφός); fond of wise things, i.e. a "philosopher".

G5387.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and (cherishing one's kindred, especially parents or children); fond of natural relatives, i.e. fraternal towards fellow Christian.

G5388.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G5043 (τέκνον); fond of one's children, i.e. maternal.

G5389.def: middle voice from a compound of G5384 (φίλος) and G5092 (τιμή); to be fond of honor, i.e. emulous (eager or earnest to do something)

G5390.def: adverb from G5391 (φιλόφρων); with friendliness of mind, i.e. kindly.

G5391.def: from G5384 (φίλος) and G5424 (φρήν); friendly of mind, i.e. kind.

G5393.def: active participle of the base of G5395 (φλόξ); blazing; Phlegon, a Christian.

G5394.def: from G5395 (φλόξ); to cause a blaze, i.e. ignite (figuratively, to inflame with passion)

G5395.def: from a primary (to "flash" or "flame"); a blaze.

G5396.def: from G5397 (φλύαρος); to be a babbler or trifler, i.e. (by implication) to berate idly or mischievously.

G5397.def: from (to bubble); a garrulous person, i.e. prater.

G5399.def: from G5401 (φόβος); to frighten, i.e. (passively) to be alarmed; by analogy, to be in awe of, i.e. revere.

G5400.def: neuter of a derivative of G5399 (φοβέω); a frightening thing, i.e. terrific portent.

G5401.def: from a primary (to be put in fear); alarm or fright.

G5402.def: feminine of (bright; probably akin to the base of G5457 (φῶς)); Phœbe, a Christian woman.

G5403.def: from G5404 (φοῖνιξ); palm-country; Phœnice (or Phœnicia), a region of Palestine.

G5405.def: probably the same as G5404 (φοῖνιξ); Phœnix, a place in Crete.

G5406.def: from G5408 (φόνος); a murderer (always of criminal (or at least intentional) homicide; which G443 (ἀνθρωποκτόνος) does not necessarily imply; while G4607 (σικάριος) is a special term for a public bandit)

G5407.def: from G5406 (φονεύς); to be a murderer (of)

G5408.def: from an obsolete primary (to slay); murder.

G5409.def: from G5411 (φόρος); to have a burden, i.e. (by analogy) to wear as clothing or a constant accompaniment.

G5410.def: of Latin origin; a forum or market-place; only in comparative with G675 (Ἄππιος); a station on the Appian road.

G5411.def: from G5342 (φέρω); a load (as borne), i.e. (figuratively) a tax (properly, an individual assessment on persons or property; whereas G5056 (τέλος) is usually a general toll on goods or travel)

G5412.def: from G5414 (φόρτος); to load up (properly, as a vessel or animal), i.e. (figuratively) to overburden with ceremony (or spiritual anxiety)

G5413.def: diminutive of G5414 (φόρτος); an invoice (as part of freight), i.e. (figuratively) a task or service.

G5414.def: from G5342 (φέρω); something carried, i.e. the cargo of a ship.

G5415.def: of Latin origin; "fortunate"; Fortunatus, a Christian.

G5416.def: neuter of a derivative from the base of G5417 (φραγελλόω); a whip, i.e. Roman lash as a public punishment.

G5417.def: from a presumed equivalent of the Latin flagellum; to whip, i.e. lash as a public punishment.

G5418.def: from G5420 (φράσσω); a fence, or inclosing barrier (literally or figuratively)

G5419.def: probably akin to G5420 (φράσσω) through the idea of defining; to indicate (by word or act), i.e. (specially), to expound.

G5420.def: apparently a strengthening form of the base of G5424 (φρήν); to fence or inclose, i.e. (specially), to block up (figuratively, to silence)

G5421.def: of uncertain derivation; a hole in the ground (dug for obtaining or holding water or other purposes), i.e. a cistern or well; figuratively, an abyss (as a prison)

G5422.def: from G5423 (φρεναπάτης); to be a mind-misleader, i.e. delude.

G5423.def: from G5424 (φρήν) and G539 (ἀπάτη); a mind-misleader, i.e. seducer.

G5424.def: probably from an obsolete (to rein in or curb; compare G5420 (φράσσω)); the midrif (as a partition of the body), i.e. (figuratively and by implication, of sympathy) the feelings (or sensitive nature; by extension (also in the plural) the mind or cognitive faculties)

G5425.def: apparently a primary verb; to "bristle" or chill, i.e. shudder (fear)

G5426.def: from G5424 (φρήν); to exercise the mind, i.e. entertain or have a sentiment or opinion; by implication, to be (mentally) disposed (more or less earnestly in a certain direction); intensively, to interest oneself in (with concern or obedience)
G5426.kjv: set the affection on, (be) care(-ful), (be like-, + be of one, + be of the same, + let this) mind(-ed), regard, savour, think

G5428.def: from G5426 (φρονέω); mental action or activity, i.e. intellectual or moral insight.

G5429.def: from G5424 (φρήν); thoughtful, i.e. sagacious or discreet (implying a cautious character; while G4680 (σοφός) denotes practical skill or acumen; and G4908 (συνετός) indicates rather intelligence or mental acquirement); in a bad sense conceited (also in the comparative)

G5431.def: from a derivative of G5424 (φρήν); to exercise thought, i.e. be anxious.

G5432.def: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G3708 (ὁράω); to be a watcher in advance, i.e. to mount guard as a sentinel (post spies at gates); figuratively, to hem in, protect.

G5433.def: akin to G1032 (βρύω), G1031 (βρύχω); to snort (as a spirited horse), i.e. (figuratively) to make a tumult.

G5434.def: neuter of a presumed derivative of (to roast or parch; akin to the base of G5395 (φλόξ)); something desiccated, i.e. a dry twig.

G5435.def: probably of foreign origin; Phrygia, a region of Asia Minor.

G5436.def: probably from G5343 (φεύγω); fugitive; Phygellus, an apostate Christian.

G5437.def: from G5343 (φεύγω); a fleeing, i.e. escape.

G5438.def: from G5442 (φυλάσσω); a guarding or (concretely, guard), the act, the person; figuratively, the place, the condition, or (specially), the time (as a division of day or night), literally or figuratively.

G5440.def: neuter of a derivative of G5442 (φυλάσσω); a guard-case, i.e. "phylactery" for wearing slips of Scripture texts.

G5441.def: from G5442 (φυλάσσω); a watcher or sentry.

G5442.def: probably from G5443 (φυλή) through the idea of isolation; to watch, i.e. be on guard (literally of figuratively); by implication, to preserve, obey, avoid.

G5443.def: from G5453 (φύω) (compare G5444 (φύλλον)); an offshoot, i.e. race or clan.

G5444.def: from the same as G5443 (φυλή); a sprout, i.e. leaf.

G5445.def: from a prolonged form of (to mix a liquid with a solid; perhaps akin to G5453 (φύω) through the idea of swelling in bulk), mean to knead; a mass of dough.

G5447.def: adverb from G5446 (φυσικός); "physically", i.e. (by implication) instinctively.

G5448.def: from G5449 (φύσις) in the primary sense of blowing; to inflate, i.e. (figuratively) make proud (haughty)

G5449.def: from G5453 (φύω); growth (by germination or expansion), i.e. (by implication) natural production (lineal descent); by extension, a genus or sort; figuratively, native disposition, constitution or usage.

G5451.def: from G5452 (φυτεύω); trans-planting, i.e. (concretely) a shrub or vegetable.

G5452.def: from a derivative of G5453 (φύω); to set out in the earth, i.e. implant; figuratively, to instil doctrine.

G5453.def: a primary verb; probably originally, to "puff" or blow, i.e. to swell up; but only used in the implied sense, to germinate or grow (sprout, produce), literally or figuratively.

G5454.def: of uncertain derivative; a burrow or lurking-place.

G5455.def: from G5456 (φωνή); to emit a sound (animal, human or instrumental); by implication, to address in words or by name, also in imitation.

G5456.def: probably akin to G5316 (φαίνω) through the idea of disclosure; a tone (articulate, bestial or artificial); by implication, an address (for any purpose), saying or language.

G5457.def: from an obsolete φάω (to shine or make manifest, especially by rays); luminousness (in the widest application, natural or artificial, abstract or concrete, literal or figurative).

G5458.def: from G5457 (φῶς); an illuminator, i.e. (concretely) a luminary, or (abstractly) brilliancy.

G5459.def: from G5457 (φῶς) and G5342 (φέρω); light-bearing ("phosphorus"), i.e. (specially), the morning-star (figuratively)

G5460.def: from G5457 (φῶς); lustrous, i.e. transparent or well-illuminated (figuratively)

G5461.def: from G5457 (φῶς); to shed rays, i.e. to shine or (transitively) to brighten up (literally or figuratively)
G5461.kjv: enlighten, illuminate, (bring to, give) light, make to see

G5463.def: a primary verb; to be "cheer"ful, i.e. calmly happy or well-off; impersonally, especially as salutation (on meeting or parting), be well.
G5463.kjv: farewell, be glad, God speed, greeting, hall, joy(- fully), rejoice

G5465.def: from the base of G5490 (χάσμα); to lower (as into a void)

G5466.def: probably of Hebrew or (H03778); a Chaldæan (i.e. Kasdi), or native or the region of the lower Euphrates.

G5467.def: perhaps from G5465 (χαλάω) through the idea of reducing the strength; difficult, i.e. dangerous, or (by implication) furious.

G5468.def: from a compound of G5469 (χαλινός) and the reduplicated form of G71 (ἄγω); to be a bit-leader, i.e. to curb (figuratively)

G5469.def: from G5465 (χαλάω); a curb or head-stall (as curbing the spirit)

G5471.def: from G5475 (χαλκός); a copper-worker or brazier.

G5472.def: from G5475 (χαλκός) and perhaps G1491 (εἶδος); copper-like, i.e. "chalcedony".

G5473.def: diminutive from G5475 (χαλκός); a copper dish.

G5474.def: neuter of a compound of G5475 (χαλκός) and G3030 (λίβανος) (in the implied mean of whiteness or brilliancy); burnished copper, an alloy of copper (or gold) and silver having a brilliant lustre.

G5475.def: perhaps from G5465 (χαλάω) through the idea of hollowing out as a vessel (this metal being chiefly used for that purpose); copper (the substance, or some implement or coin made of it)

G5476.def: adverb perhaps from the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of a fissure in the soil; earthward, i.e. prostrate.

G5477.def: of Hebrew origin (H03667); Chanaan (i.e. Kenaan), the early name of Palestine.

G5478.def: from G5477 (Χαναάν); a Chanaanæan (i.e. Kenaanite), or native of gentile Palestine.

G5479.def: from G5463 (χαίρω); cheerfulness, i.e. calm delight.
G5479.kjv: gladness, X greatly, (X be exceeding) joy(-ful, -fully, -fulness, -ous)

G5480.def: from the same as G5482 (χάραξ); a scratch or etching, i.e. stamp (as a badge of servitude), or scupltured figure (statue)

G5481.def: from the same as G5482 (χάραξ); a graver (the tool or the person), i.e. (by implication) engraving (("character"), the figure stamped, i.e. an exact copy or (figuratively) representation)

G5482.def: from (to sharpen to a point; akin to G1125 (γράφω) through the idea of scratching); a stake, i.e. (by implication) a palisade or rampart (military mound for circumvallation in a siege)

G5483.def: middle voice from G5485 (χάρις); to grant as a favor, i.e. gratuitously, in kindness, pardon or rescue.

G5484.def: accusative case of G5485 (χάρις) as preposition; through favor of, i.e. on account of.
G5484.kjv: be-(for) cause of, for sake of, +…fore, X reproachfully

G5485.def: from G5463; graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude).

G5486.def: from G5483 (χαρίζομαι); a (divine) gratuity, i.e. deliverance (from danger or passion); (specially), a (spiritual) endowment, i.e. (subjectively) religious qualification, or (objectively) miraculous faculty.

G5487.def: from G5485 (χάρις); to grace, i.e. indue with special honor.

G5488.def: of Hebrew origin (H02771); Charrhan (i.e. Charan), a place in Mesopotamia.

G5489.def: from the same as G5482 (χάραξ); a sheet ("chart") of writing-material (as to be scribbled over)

G5490.def: from a form of an obsolete primary (to "gape" or "yawn"); a "chasm" or vacancy (impassable interval)

G5491.def: from a form of the same as G5490 (χάσμα); a lip (as a pouring place); figuratively, a margin (of water)

G5492.def: from the same as G5494 (χειμών); to storm, i.e. (passively) to labor under a gale.

G5493.def: from the base of G5494 (χειμών) and G4482 (ῥέω); a storm-runlet, i.e. winter-torrent.

G5494.def: from a derivative of (to pour; akin to the base of 5490 through the idea of a channel), meaning a storm (as pouring rain); by implication, the rainy season, i.e. winter.

G5495.def: perhaps from the base of G5494 (χειμών) in the sense of its congener the base of G5490 (χάσμα) (through the idea of hollowness for grasping); the hand (literally or figuratively (power); especially (by Hebraism) a means or instrument)

G5496.def: from G5497 (χειραγωγός); to be a hand-leader, i.e. to guide (a blind person)

G5497.def: from G5495 (χείρ) and a reduplicated form of G71 (ἄγω); a hand-leader, i.e. personal conductor (of a blind person)
G5497.kjv: some to lead by the hand

G5498.def: neuter of a compound of G5495 (χείρ) and G1125 (γράφω); something hand-written ("chirograph"), i.e. a manuscript (specially, a legal document or bond (figuratively))

G5499.def: from G5495 (χείρ) and a derivative of G4160 (ποιέω); manufactured, i.e. of human construction.

G5500.def: from a comparative of G5495 (χείρ) and (to stretch); to be a hand-reacher or voter (by raising the hand), i.e. (generally) to select or appoint.

G5501.def: irregular comparative of G2556 (κακός); from an obsolete equivalent (of uncertain derivation); more evil or aggravated (physically, mentally or morally)

G5502.def: plural of Hebrew origin (H03742); "cherubim" (i.e. cherubs or kerubim)

G5503.def: feminine of a presumed derivative apparently from the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of deficiency; a widow (as lacking a husband), literally or figuratively.

G5504.def: of uncertain derivation; "yesterday"; by extension, in time past or hitherto.

G5506.def: from G5507 (χίλιοι) and G757 (ἄρχω); the commander of a thousand soldiers ("chiliarch"; i.e. colonel.

G5507.def: plural of uncertain affinity; a thousand.

G5508.def: of uncertain derivation; Chios, an island in the Mediterranean.

G5509.def: of foreign origin (H03801); a tunic or shirt.

G5510.def: perhaps akin to the base of G5490 (χάσμα) (G5465 (χαλάω)) or G5494 (χειμών) (as descending or empty); snow.

G5511.def: of uncertain derivation; a military cloak.

G5512.def: from a derivative probably of G5491 (χεῖλος); to throw out the lip, i.e. jeer at.

G5514.def: feminine of apparently a primary word; "green"; Chloë, a Christian female.

G5515.def: from the same as G5514 (Χλόη); greenish, i.e. verdant, dun-colored.

G5516.def: the 22nd, 14th and an obsolete letter (G4742 (στίγμα) as a cross) of the Greek alphabet (intermediate between the 5th and 6th), used as numbers; denoting respectively 600, 60 and 6; 666 as a numeral.

G5517.def: from G5522 (χόος); dusty or dirty (soil-like), i.e. (by implication) terrene.

G5518.def: of uncertain derivation; a chœnix or certain dry measure.

G5520.def: from G5521 (χολή); to be bilious, i.e. (by implication) irritable (enraged, "choleric")

G5521.def: feminine of an equivalent perhaps akin to the same as 5514 (from the greenish hue); "gall" or bile, i.e. (by analogy) poison or an anodyne (wormwood, poppy, etc.)

G5522.def: from the base of G5494 (χειμών); a heap (as poured out), i.e. rubbish; loose dirt.

G5523.def: of uncertain derivation; Chorazin, a place in Palestine.

G5524.def: from a compound of G5525 (χορός) and G71 (ἄγω); to be a dance-leader, i.e. (generally) to furnish.

G5525.def: of uncertain derivation; a ring, i.e. round dance ("choir".

G5526.def: from G5528 (χόρτος); to fodder, i.e. (generally) to gorge (supply food in abundance)

G5528.def: apparently a primary word; a "court" or "garden", i.e. (by implication, of pasture) herbage or vegetation.

G5529.def: of uncertain origin; Chuzas, an officer of Herod.

G5530.def: middle voice of a primary verb (perhaps rather from G5495 (χείρ), to handle); to furnish what is needed; (give an oracle, "graze" (touch slightly), light upon, etc.), i.e. (by implication) to employ or (by extension) to act towards one in a given manner.

G5531.def: probably the same as the base of G5530 (χράομαι); to loan.

G5532.def: from the base of G5530 (χράομαι) or G5534 (χρή); employment, i.e. an affair; also (by implication) occasion, demand, requirement or destitution.
G5532.kjv: business, lack, necessary(-ity), need(-ful), use, want

G5533.def: from a derivative of G5531 (χράω) and G3781 (ὀφειλέτης); a loan-ower, i.e. indebted person.

G5534.def: third person singular of the same as G5530 (χράομαι) or G5531 (χράω) used impersonally; it needs (must or should) be.

G5535.def: from G5532 (χρεία); to make (i.e. have) necessity, i.e. be in want of.

G5536.def: something useful or needed, i.e. wealth, price.

G5537.def: from G5536 (χρῆμα); to utter an oracle (compare the original sense of G5530 (χράομαι)), i.e. divinely intimate; by implication, (compare the secular sense of G5532 (χρεία)) to constitute a firm for business, i.e. (generally) bear as a title.
G5537.kjv: be called, be admonished (warned) of God, reveal, speak

G5538.def: from G5537 (χρηματίζω); a divine response or revelation.

G5540.def: from G5530 (χράομαι); employment, i.e. (specially), sexual intercourse (as an occupation of the body)

G5541.def: middle voice from G5543 (χρηστός); to show oneself useful, i.e. act benevolently.

G5542.def: from a compound of G5543 (χρηστός) and G3004 (λέγω); fair speech, i.e. plausibility.

G5543.def: from G5530 (χράομαι); employed, i.e. (by implication) useful (in manner or morals)

G5544.def: from G5543 (χρηστός); usefulness, i.e. morally, excellence (in character or demeanor)

G5545.def: from G5548 (χρίω); an unguent or smearing, i.e. (figuratively) the special endowment ("chrism") of the Holy Spirit.

G5546.def: from G5547 (Χριστός); a Christian, i.e. follower of Christ.

G5547.def: from G5548; anointed, i.e., the Messiah, an epithet of Jesus.

G5548.def: probably akin to G5530 (χράομαι) through the idea of contact; to smear or rub with oil, i.e. (by implication) to consecrate to an office or religious service.

G5549.def: from G5550 (χρόνος); to take time, i.e. linger.

G5550.def: of uncertain derivation; a space of time (in general, and thus properly distinguished from G2540 (καιρός), which designates a fixed or special occasion; and from G165 (αἰών), which denotes a particular period) or interval; by extension, an individual opportunity; by implication, delay.
G5550.kjv: + years old, season, space, (X often-)time(-s), (a) while

G5551.def: from a presumed compound of G5550 (χρόνος) and the base of G5147 (τρίβος); to be a time-wearer, i.e. to procrastinate (linger)

G5553.def: diminutive of G5557 (χρυσός); a golden article, i.e. gold plating, ornament, or coin.

G5554.def: from G5557 (χρυσός) and G1146 (δακτύλιος); gold-ringed, i.e. wearing a golden finger-ring or similar jewelry.

G5555.def: from G5557 (χρυσός) and G3037 (λίθος); gold-stone, i.e. a yellow gem ("chrysolite")

G5556.def: from G5557 (χρυσός) and (a leek); a greenish-yellow gem ("chrysoprase")

G5557.def: perhaps from the base of G5530 (χράομαι) (through the idea of the utility of the metal); gold; by extension, a golden article, as an ornament or coin.

G5558.def: from G5557 (χρυσός); to gild, i.e. bespangle with golden ornaments.

G5559.def: probably akin to the base of G5530 (χράομαι) through the idea of handling; the body (properly, its surface or skin)

G5560.def: apparently a primary word; "halt", i.e. limping.

G5561.def: feminine of a derivative of the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of empty expanse; room, i.e. a space of territory (more or less extensive; often including its inhabitants)
G5561.kjv: coast, county, fields, ground, land, region

G5562.def: from G5561 (χώρα); to be in (give) space, i.e. (intransitively) to pass, enter, or (transitively) to hold, admit (literally or figuratively)
G5562.kjv: come, contain, go, have place, (can, be room to) receive

G5563.def: from G5561 (χώρα); to place room between, i.e. part; reflexively, to go away.

G5564.def: diminutive of G5561 (χώρα); a spot or plot of ground.
G5564.kjv: field, land, parcel of ground, place, possession

G5565.def: adverb from G5561 (χώρα); at a space, i.e. separately or apart from (often as preposition)

G5566.def: of Latin origin; the north-west wind.

G5567.def: probably strengthened from (to rub or touch the surface; compare G5597 (ψώχω)); to twitch or twang, i.e. to play on a stringed instrument (celebrate the divine worship with music and accompanying odes)

G5568.def: from G5567 (ψάλλω); a set piece of music, i.e. a sacred ode (accompanied with the voice, harp or other instrument; a "psalm"); collectively, the book of the Psalms.

G5569.def: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G80 (ἀδελφός); a spurious brother, i.e. pretended associate.

G5570.def: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G652 (ἀπόστολος); a spurious apostle, i.e. pretended pracher.

G5571.def: from G5574 (ψεύδομαι); untrue, i.e. erroneous, deceitful, wicked.

G5572.def: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G1320 (διδάσκαλος); a spurious teacher, i.e. propagator of erroneous Christian doctrine.

G5573.def: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G3004 (λέγω); mendacious, i.e. promulgating erroneous Christian doctrine.

G5574.def: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to utter an untruth or attempt to deceive by falsehood.

G5575.def: from G5571 (ψευδής) and a kindred form of G3144 (μάρτυς); a spurious witness, i.e. bearer of untrue testimony.

G5576.def: from G5575 (ψευδομάρτυρ); to be an untrue testifier, i.e. offer falsehood in evidence.

G5578.def: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G4396 (προφήτης); a spurious prophet, i.e. pretended foreteller or religious impostor.

G5580.def: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G5547 (Χριστός); a spurious Messiah.

G5581.def: from G5571 (ψευδής) and G3686 (ὄνομα); untruly named.

G5582.def: from G5574 (ψεύδομαι); a fabrication, i.e. falsehood.

G5584.def: from the base of G5567 (ψάλλω) (compare G5586 (ψῆφος)); to manipulate, i.e. verify by contact; figuratively, to search for.

G5585.def: from G5586 (ψῆφος); to use pebbles in enumeration, i.e. (generally) to compute.

G5586.def: from the same as G5584 (ψηλαφάω); a pebble (as worn smooth by handling), i.e. (by implication, of use as a counter or ballot) a verdict (of acquittal) or ticket (of admission); a vote.

G5587.def: from a derivative of (a whisper; by implication, a slander; probably akin to G5574 (ψεύδομαι)); whispering, i.e. secret detraction.

G5588.def: from the same as G5587 (ψιθυρισμός); a secret calumniator.

G5589.def: diminutive from a derivative of the base of G5567 (ψάλλω) (meaning a crumb); a little bit or morsel.

G5590.def: from G5594; breath, i.e., (by implication) spirit, abstractly or concretely (the animal sentient principle only; thus distinguished on the one hand from G4151, which is the rational and immortal soul; and on the other from G2222, which is mere vitality, even of plants).

G5591.def: from G5590 (ψυχή); sensitive, i.e. animate (in distinction on the one hand from G4152 (πνευματικός), which is the higher or renovated nature; and on the other from G5446 (φυσικός), which is the lower or bestial nature)

G5594.def: a primary verb; to breathe (voluntarily but gently, thus differing on the one hand from G4154 (πνέω), which denotes properly a forcible respiration; and on the other from the base of G109 (ἀήρ), which refers properly to an inanimate breeze), i.e. (by implication, of reduction of temperature by evaporation) to chill (figuratively)

G5595.def: from the base of G5596 (ψωμίον); to supply with bits, i.e. (generally) to nourish.

G5596.def: diminutive from a derivative of the base of G5597 (ψώχω); a crumb or morsel (as if rubbed off), i.e. a mouthful.

G5597.def: prolongation from the same base as G5567 (ψάλλω); to triturate, i.e. (by analogy) to rub out (kernels from husks with the fingers or hand)

G5598.def: the last letter of the Greek alphabet, i.e. (figuratively) the finality.

G5599.def: a primary interjection; as a sign of the vocative case, O; as a note of exclamation, oh.

G5600.def: the subjunctive of G1510 (εἰμί); (may, might, can, could, would, should, must, etc.; also with G1487 (εἰ) and its comparative, as well as with other particles) be.
G5600.kjv: + appear, are, (may, might, should) be, X have, is, + pass the flower of her age, should stand, were

G5601.def: of Hebrew origin (H05744); Obed, an Israelite.

G5602.def: from an adverb form of G3592 (ὅδε); in this same spot, i.e. here or hither.

G5603.def: from G103 (ᾄδω); a chant or "ode" (the general term for any words sung; while G5215 (ὕμνος) denotes especially a religious metrical composition, and G5568 (ψαλμός) still more specially, a Hebrew cantillation)

G5604.def: akin to G3601 (ὀδύνη); a pang or throe, especially of childbirth.

G5605.def: from G5604 (ὠδίν); to experience the pains of parturition (literally or figuratively)

G5606.def: perhaps from the alternate of G5342 (φέρω); the shoulder (as that on which burdens are borne)

G5608.def: middle voice from an apparently primary (a sum or price); to purchase (synonymous with the earlier G4092 (πίμπρημι))

G5610.def: apparently a primary word; an 'hour' (literally or figuratively).

G5611.def: from G5610 (ὥρα); belonging to the right hour or season (timely), i.e. (by implication) flourishing (beauteous (figuratively))

G5612.def: middle voice of an apparently primary verb; to "roar".

G5613.def: probably adverb of comparative from G3739 (ὅς); which how, i.e. in that manner (very variously used, as follows)
G5613.kjv: about, after (that), (according) as (it had been, it were), as soon (as), even as (like), for, how (greatly), like (as, unto), since, so (that), that, to wit, unto, when(-soever), while, X with all speed

G5614.def: of Hebrew origin (H03467 and H04994); oh save!; hosanna (i.e. hoshia-na), an exclamation of adoration.

G5615.def: from G5613 (ὡς) and an adverb from G846 (αὐτός); as thus, i.e. in the same way.
G5615.kjv: even so, likewise, after the same (in like) manner

G5616.def: from G5613 (ὡς) and G1487 (εἰ); as if.
G5616.kjv: about, as (it had been, it were), like (as)

G5617.def: of Hebrew origin (H01954); Hosee (i.e. Hoshea), an Israelite.

G5618.def: from G5613 (ὡς) and G4007 (περ); just as, i.e. exactly like.

G5619.def: from G5618 (ὥσπερ) and G1487 (εἰ); just as if, i.e. as it were.

G5620.def: from G5613 (ὡς) and G5037 (τέ); so too, i.e. thus therefore (in various relations of consecution, as follow)
G5620.kjv: (insomuch) as, so that (then), (insomuch) that, therefore, to, wherefore

G5621.def: diminutive of G3775 (οὖς); an earlet, i.e. one of the ears, or perhaps the lobe of the ear.

G5622.def: from a derivative of the base of G5624 (ὠφέλιμος); usefulness, i.e. benefit.

G5623.def: from the same as G5622 (ὠφέλεια); to be useful, i.e. to benefit.

G5624.def: from a form of G3786 (ὄφελος); helpful or serviceable, i.e. advantageous.
